title: Front Office Assistant
description: **JOB TITLE:** FRONT OFFICE ASSISTANT **RESPONSIBLE FOR:** To greet guests in accordance with training and allocate rooms according to laid\-down procedures. The main duties involved in the role of a Front Office Assistant at The Address Collective are outlined below however this list is not exhaustive. **Main Duties** * To arrive promptly on duty as scheduled in a full, clean \& presentable uniform at all times in accordance with company grooming policy. * Maintain the highest level of personal and work cleanliness and hygiene. * Adhere to the company's Code of Conduct. * To comply with company regulations regarding, fire, health and safety, hygiene, customer care and security. * To read, understand and carry out your responsibilities as defined in the Health and Safety Statement and Staff Handbook. * Check and ensure the correctness of all reception floats. * Ensure work areas are kept clean, safe and tidy at all times. * Receive and register guests on arrival, using their names at all times adhering to laid down procedures e.g. brand standards. * Ensure all documentation relating to the registration and checking out of guests is as per standard of procedures. * Be thoroughly familiar with all company selling procedures and promotions. * Be aware of hotel room availability and rates at all times. * Adhere to all regulations in respect of Health and Safety, hygiene, guest safety, fire regulations, emergency procedures, etc * To ensure that any additional requirements for guests are offered and catered for as much as is reasonably possible, i.e. dinner reservations, porter, information services etc. * To ensure that all charges are posted correctly onto room bills. * Maintain \& monitor management accounts. * Be aware of all hotel facilities \& amenities. (ie car parking, directions etc) * Communicate with colleagues and guests professionally. * Provide the highest level of customer services consistently. * Provide relevant reports to departments as required. * To take reservations and respond to guests in a timely and professional manner. * To answer switchboard in a warm and welcoming manner and effectively transfer calls to correct extensions in the absence of the switchboard operator. * To administer all reservations, cancellations \& no\-shows in line with company policy. * Fulfil all reasonable requests from guests to ensure their comfort, satisfaction and safety. * Conduct regular security checks throughout the day and report any issues to management. * Report any maintenance issues immediately to management, * To correctly take messages for guests/staff ensuring that date, time, message, from, to and initial are noted. * To ensure that relevant people receive any messages immediately. * Dealing with any guest requests/queries in a polite and attentive manner and reporting/logging any problems to the Duty Manager. * Balancing of telephone, lounge, restaurant, bars and reception cash at the end of each shift. * The sensible allocation of rooms to customers having viewed all relevant guest requests. * Check all telephone charges from meeting rooms and ensure they are posted to correct folios. * Handling safe deposits for guests. * Maximise in\-house selling whenever possible and maintain an awareness of all sales opportunities. * Dealing with Foreign Exchange for customers. * To ensure that all monies are kept secure at all times. * All staff of the company are required to make themselves available for training and communication meetings for which advance notice will normally be given. The content of all training session and issues addressed at team meetings must be adhered to. * Complete duties as per checklist * To ensure that all cash in dealt with in accordance with cash handling procedures. * To carry out any reasonable duty requested by a manager, senior staff member or request of a guest. **Skills** Fluent in English Outlook Hospitality

title: Front of House
description: **Title:** Front of House **Location:** Dublin 2 **Salary:** €30,000 \- €32,000 per annum **Job Type:** Permanent Full\-Time Position **Working Hours:** Monday to Friday. Flexibility is required to support occasional later evening meetings and events. **Job Summary** This position is based at a prestigious five\-star venue in Dublin City and offers an exciting opportunity to support the delivery of a high\-quality cleaning operation. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring operational excellence, maintaining exceptional client satisfaction, supporting team performance, and driving continuous improvement across the contract. Strong IT skills are essential, as the role involves reporting, scheduling, performance monitoring, and the effective use of operational systems to support day\-to\-day service delivery. **Key Responsibilities** * Take ownership of the internal and external visitor experience, ensuring all interactions leave a positive and memorable impression. * Deliver core front of house services including visitor sign\-in, badging, luggage handling, lost property management, and deliveries. * Receive, direct, and respond to telephone, email, and other front\-of\-house enquiries. * Greet, assist, and direct visitors, candidates, new hires, contractors, and members of the public professionally and efficiently. * Maintain awareness of onsite meetings and events to ensure a personalised visitor experience. * Ensure front of house, meeting rooms, and event spaces are maintained to the highest presentation standards. * Set up meeting and event spaces in accordance with operational requirements and Health \& Safety guidelines. * Monitor meeting room environments, including furniture, equipment, signage, consumables, and room conditions. * Manage and maintain room booking systems, Outlook calendars, conference room finders, and SharePoint scheduling tools. * Provide visitors and employees with information on local amenities, including restaurants, hotels, shops, and transport services. * Liaise daily with onsite partners, including security, cleaning, and facilities teams, to coordinate meetings and events. * Respond promptly and professionally to meeting and event enquiries. * Support room booking display updates and occupancy management processes. * Assist with workplace audits, site inspections, and walk\-the\-floor reviews. * Provide support across wider facilities services, including helpdesk requests, mail and courier services, administrative tasks, and ad hoc projects. * Work collaboratively across multiple workplace settings as part of a customer\-focused team. **Requirements** * Previous experience in reception, concierge, front of house, hospitality, or a related customer service role. * Experience within a corporate environment is highly desirable. * Previous catering, hospitality, or events experience would be an advantage. * Facilities Management experience or exposure is beneficial. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to engage confidently with stakeholders at all levels. * Strong customer service mindset with a proactive and solutions\-focused approach. * Fluency in English, both written and spoken. * Strong IT skills and the ability to manage multiple systems and applications effectively. * Exceptional attention to detail and organisational skills. * Ability to remain professional, approachable, and composed in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong team player with a willingness to support colleagues and contribute positively to the workplace experience. * Flexible approach to working hours to support occasional evening events and business requirements. **Inclusion Matters** We're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. At Samsic Facility, we celebrate diversity and are committed to building an inclusive environment for everyone. If you need accommodations during the application or interview process, just let us know. **Ready to take the next step?** Apply now and build your career in a role that values your precision, supports your growth, and respects your life outside of work.

title: Front of House Assistant
description: Front of House AssistantSix by Nico \- Dublin 1 Molesworth Pl, Leinster, Dublin, D02 WE52 Apply for this job **Share** As a company, we believe that we are responsible for providing a platform that will allow our employees to progress. We are proud to employ brilliant people who are passionate about what they do. We are looking for an enthusiastic **Server/Waitress/Waiter** to join the team at Six by Nico. **The role** Our front of house team plays a fundamental role in delivering a professional and informative service in a fun and exciting atmosphere; we are looking for someone who is confident and bursting with personality. With every new menu, there is an opportunity to develop your personal knowledge of food and wine as we provide in\-house training to ensure you are ready to deliver first class service that we are renowned for. **As a Server/Waitress/Waiter, Key Responsibilities Would Be** * Maintaining skills and product knowledge on current menus * Assisting head waiter/waitress on providing excellent customer service. * Assisting the kitchen team by delivering food to customers with food \& wine descriptions * Clearing tables and ensuring that appropriate cutlery is provided. * Preparing and serving drinks at the bar * Displaying enthusiasm at all times, both with customers and your colleagues. Above all, you will be a part of a fantastic hardworking team within a thriving environment with the opportunity for progression! **Preferred Requirements** * Experience in a similar position * Confident in a busy, fast paced environment * Communication skills * Can do attitude **Benefits You Can Enjoy** * Excellent opportunities for Career development and growth. * Working with a team striving for perfection daily * The opportunity to become a superstar with passion at the forefront of your professional mindset * Christmas Day \& Boxing Day guaranteed off to enjoy well deserved downtime to celebrate the festivities. * Annual Pay Review \- ensuring your skills, knowledge and experience are valued every step of the way. * Travel opportunities across all Six By Nico sites in the UK and Ireland. * Wagestream App \- giving you power over your pay, allowing you to access your salary as you earn it. * People Engagement Board \- we want to listen and learn from you, you can have this opportunity to tell us your feedback every month via our team engagement platform, we listen and learn. * Employee Assist Programme \- our employee wellbeing is integral in such a demanding industry. Every member of the team has access to “Health Assured”', a well\-being app with mental health advice and support, finance support, weekly mood tracker and more. With this programme our employees have access to support whenever they need it. * Access to Hospitality Rewards after successful completion of probationary period. **If this sounds like something you would like to be a part of, we would love to hear from you!** As a company, we believe that we are responsible for providing a platform that will allow our employees to progress. We are proud to employ brilliant people who are passionate about what they do. Salaries will be paid at an hourly rate inclusive of service charge. \#iscnfoh Apply for this job Back to Search

title: Grade V TUN IT Support
description: Overview: **Post Title****Renal Services IT Support/General Asministration** **Post Status:**Permanent **Department:** TUN Directorate **Location:**Beaumont Hospital, Dublin 9 **Reports to:****Renal Clinical Systems Nurse Manager** with regard to day to day operation**Renal Clinical Systems Nurse Manager/Directorate Support Manager,** with regard to the overall quality of work and development and compliance to hospital strategic and operational objectives **Salary:** Appointment will be made on Grade V salary scale (€52,235 \- €58,659 **€60,569, €62,485 LSIs**) at a point in line with Government pay policy. **Hours of work:**Part Time 17\.5 Hours **Closing Date:** 12\.00pm on 22/06/2026 **Job Summary:*** To assist Clinical Systems Manager in the management of the day to day operations of the Renal Clinical Management System. * Participate in the ongoing development of computer data systems for the management of Renal Services. * Train and update staff members in the correct use and benefits of the system Responsibilities: **Management of the day to day operations of the Renal System.*** Provide support to staff with regard to software and hardware problems as they arise and Liase with the Hospital IT Department. * Ensure that Renal Patients are assigned correct PAS flags/codes ie. “Nephrology”/ “Transplant” specialty set for patients encounter to enable correct download of data from the PAS. * Audit the accuracy of ADT data and laboratory results downloads from the HIS to the Renal System daily. * Daily audit of accuracy of data relating to Renal Replacement Therapy Programmes ie. Haemodialysis, CAPD, NESRD. * Examine all error reports relating to the down loads and enter data manually on the system. * Keep staff informed of any planned interruption in systems availability and ensure that manual systems are put in place during such periods. * Assist in maintaining an accurate register of all equipment in the unit and liase with the Hospital Computer Department regarding maintenance and replacement of equipment. **Staff Training*** Assist in the implementation of new computer systems and training schedules for staff, as appropriate. * Implement training for all newly appointed staff and training update in accordance with established procedures. * Provide ongoing support and evaluate effectiveness of training programme. **Security of the System*** Enforce access security procedures for end users in accordance with established procedures. * Set\-up and maintain user identity codes and passwords and security access levels, as appropriate, for newly appointed staff and disable codes of staff who have terminated. * Have a working knowledge and be aware of implications of the Data Protection Act and the Freedom of Information Act and ensure that these issues form part of the training package. * Be aware of the Beaumont Hospital Policy on the use of Electronic Mail, Fax Services and Internet. * Record and follow\-up on all incidents relating to system breakdown and loss of data. * Ensure the security of equipment. * Maintain confidentiality at all times in all aspects of work. **Development and Implementation of Procedures for Collating Clinical** **and Auditing Data.*** Assist in maintaining and developing systems for collating ESRD diagnostic an outcome data including mortality rates. * Assist in the ongoing development of Beaumont Hospital Renal Registry and potential links with National Renal Registry initiatives. * Provide reports to facilitate clinical audit and monthly statistical analysis of workload. * Responsible for the collation and analysis of Renal ADT Activity, Renal Day Care and Out Patients. **Communications*** Demonstrate good communication skills and work closely with IT Support Manager. * Attend and take minutes of M\&M meetings/Clinical Vision Group Meetings. * Encourage good multi\-disciplinary communication with regard to utilisation of system and communicate with IT Support Nurses **Training*** It is recognised that induction training will be necessary to introduce and familiarize the post holder with the clerical services and also to provide him/her with an understanding of the integrated programmes of care provided to patients. * The post holder will be expected to attend systems development and training workshops appropriate to his/her job duties. * He/she will also undertake any further training which the Directorate Support Manager and Renal Clinical Systems Nurse Manager considers necessary for the effective implementation of the job role. Qualifications: *Selection criteria outline the qualifications, skills, knowledge and/or experience that the successful candidate would need to demonstrate for successful discharge of the responsibilities of the post.**Applications will be assessed on the basis of how well candidates satisfy these criteria.* **Mandatory:** Candidates must have at the latest date of application: **1\. Professional Qualifications, Experience, etc** (a) Eligible applicants will be those who on the closing date for the competition: (i) Have satisfactory experience as a Clerical Officer in the HSE, TUSLA, other statutory health agencies, or a body which provides services on behalf of the HSE under Section 38 of the Health Act 2004\. **Or** (ii) Have obtained a pass (Grade D) in at least five subjects from the approved list of subjects in the Department of Education Leaving Certificate Examination, including Mathematics and English or Irish1 . Candidates should have obtained at least Grade C on higher level papers in three subjects in that examination. **Or** (iii) Have completed a relevant examination at a comparable standard in any equivalent examination in another jurisdiction **Or** (iv) Hold a comparable and relevant third level qualification of at least level 6 on the National Qualifications Framework maintained by Qualifications and Quality Ireland, (QQI). Candidates must achieve a pass in Ordinary or Higher level papers. A pass in a foundation level paper is not acceptable. Candidates must have achieved these grades on the Leaving Certificate Established programme or the Leaving Certificate Vocational programme. The Leaving Certification Applied Programme does not fulfil the eligibility criteria (b) Candidates must possess the requisite knowledge and ability, including a high standard of suitability, for the proper discharge of the office. **2\. Age** Age restriction shall only apply to a candidate where s/he is not classified as a new entrant (within the meaning of the Public Service Superannuation (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, 2004\). A candidate who is not classified as a new entrant must be under 65 years of age on the first day of the month in which the latest date for receiving completed application forms for the office occurs. **3\. Health** Candidates for and any person holding the office must be fully competent and capable of undertaking the duties attached to the office and be in a state of health such as would indicate a reasonable prospect of ability to render regular and efficient service. **4\. Character** Candidates for and any person holding the office must be of good character. **Desirable:****(i)** Have, on the latest date for receiving completed applications, at least two years satisfactory experience in a designated office under a health board now known as Health Services Executive, local authority or vocational education committee in the State or the General Medical Services (Payments) Board, St. James’s Hospital Board, Beaumont Hospital Board, An Bord Altranais or the Local Government Computer Services Board And **(ii)** Possess sufficient administrative capacity to discharge the functions of the grade.*Ideally* candidates should have * Experience in a supervisory role which includes day to day people management * Knowledge of Beaumont Hospital Strategy and Organisational Development objectives * Knowledge of hospital information systems * Excellent interpersonal skills/communication skills * Ability to work as part of team Informal Enquiries ONLY to: (Please note NO APPLICATIONS will be accepted via the Informal Enquiries email address) **Name:** Claire McElligott **Title :** Directorate Support Manager **Email address:** Clairemcelligott@beaumont.ie

title: Graduate Building Surveyor
description: Graduate / APC Level Building Surveyor \- North Dublin Our client is a leading, multi\-disciplinary practice providing premium residential and commercial property solutions across Dublin and the surrounding counties. Operating from a North Dublin office they have a team of experienced Surveyors. They can provide a structured, supportive, and practical environment designed to help early\-career surveyors rapidly develop their skills and successfully achieve Chartership. **Role Overview** They are seeking an ambitious and enthusiastic **Graduate through to APC\-Level Building Surveyors** to join our growing North Dublin team. In this role, you will gain hands\-on, diverse experience across both the residential and commercial sectors. Working directly alongside Directors and Senior Chartered Surveyors, you will be fully supported through the **SCSI / RICS Assessment of Professional Competence (APC)** pathway, with access to diverse project work and structured mentorship to help you pass your diary and final assessment. * **Location:** North Dublin with site travel. * **Salary:** Competitive (Commensurate with experience) \+ APC support package. * **Reporting to:** Director **Key Responsibilities** In tis role you will assist with and gradually take ownership of a broad range of building surveying instructions, including: **Professional \& Technical Services** * Carrying out residential Pre\-Purchase House Surveys and Homebuyer Surveys. * Conducting detailed Snag Inspections and compiling comprehensive Snag List Reports for new\-build residential developments. * Assisting with Commercial Building Surveys, Schedule of Conditions, and Dilapidations assessments. * Conducting measured building surveys using advanced electronic surveying/scanning software to generate accurate real\-time floor plans. **Contract Administration \& Design** * Assisting with the design, specification, and contract administration of refurbishment, repair, and planned maintenance projects. * Contributing to Planning Applications and building control compliance tracking. * Preparing Insurance Rebuilding and Reinstatement Cost Assessments (BCIS/SCSI guidelines). * Undertaking Land Registry and boundary mapping surveys. **Client \& Progress Management** * Drafting high\-quality, technically accurate professional reports for private individuals, corporate clients, financial institutions, and solicitors. * Managing client communications professionally, responding to enquiries, and updating the internal tracking systems. **Required Qualifications \& Experience** * **Education:** A BSc (Hons) degree in Building Surveying (ideally from an SCSI/RICS accredited course). * **Experience:** Open to recent graduates, as well as those with 1–3 years of post\-graduate experience actively working through their APC diary. * **Technical Aptitude:** Strong IT literacy, including familiarity with AutoCAD and modern digital surveying equipment/apps. * **Knowledge:** A solid foundation in construction technology, building pathology, and Irish Building Regulations. * **Driving License:** A full, clean Irish/EU driving license and access to a car is **essential** for site visits across Dublin and surrounding counties. **Key Competencies** **Commitment to Learning:** A clear drive to pursue and complete the APC process to become a Chartered Building Surveyor. **Attention to Detail:** An analytical eye for spotting building defects, poor workmanship, and structural anomalies. **Good Communication Skills:** Excellent verbal and written communication skills to write robust technical reports and communicate clearly with clients. **On Offer** * **Full APC Structured Support:** Dedicated mentorship from registered SCSI/RICS supervisors and counselors, structured diary rotation, and mock interview preparation. * **Diverse Case Load:** Exposure to both residential and commercial projects, ensuring a well\-rounded foundation for your career and your APC submissions. * **Professional Fees Paid:** SCSI/RICS professional membership and subscription fees covered. * **Progression:** A clear pathway to a Post\-Chartered Surveyor role within the firm upon successful completion of your APC. * **Culture:** A collaborative, professional, and friendly working environment in North Dublin with free parking on site. If you are a building surveyor looking for your next challenge then this could be a good role for you. Please share your latest CV to rhughes@macdonaldandcompany.com

title: Graduate CSA Engineer
description: Graduate CSA Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres and Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Roles\& Responnsibilities** * Management of the CSA package. * Management of Site Safety in conjunction with the project manager and the Mercury Safety Advisor. This will include the setup, management of and closing out of the safety file as part of the Management team. * Management of Site QA with regards to the CSA works on site ensuring that a robust system is in place to avoid future issues. Record and documents all facets of the works. * Management of Project Schedule in conjunction with Mercury Project manager, consulting Engineer and Client requirements * Management of all day to day site operations * Ensure Mercury Policies and procedures are followed closely. * Management of sub\-contractor activities including tracking progress against schedule and manpower levels. Ensure that there is, and you attend, a weekly minuted meeting with sub\-contractor. * Management of the Mercury “In Progress” snagging system ensuring that the supervisors return weekly punch lists where applicable. Ensure all snags are distributed to all Sub\-contractors * Issue direct labour hours and sub\-contractor hours to time administrator for tracking centrally. * Attendance at Site Meetings, Internal Project Reviews, Engineering Reviews, Planning and Co\-ordination Meetings and Handover Meetings * Close out of all project items including O \& M Manuals, Punch List, Safety File, QA File, Project Plan – all with the support of the site project manager and your project team. * Issue internal weekly progress report, to the project manager, highlighting areas not available, delays, shortage of design, third party hold ups and percentage complete. * Maintain a weekly project photographic diary * All other duties associated with this role and/or reasonably required by the Company the Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to take into account changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential** **Selection Criteria / Person Specification:** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& coordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background Apply Now

title: Graduate Quantity Surveyor
description: Join us to help lead the next wave of the digital economy. Our Graduate Programme is a structured two\-year programme that will equip you with the skills and experience required to have a successful career at Apto. As part of this programme, we are recruiting a **Graduate Quantity Surveyor** with a commencement date in early **September 2026** and the role is based in **Dublin, Ireland** . From day one you will gain real hands\-on experience on cutting edge data centre projects while being supported and nurtured by our expert management teams. APTO Data Centres \| What we Do. Apto is a data centre company that designs, builds, and operates hyperscale data centres for our customers in high growth and emerging markets across Europe. Apto has been formed to develop and operate the European data centre assets of global investment manager, PIMCO, benefiting from access to scale, resources and land sourcing capabilities of one of the largest European real estate platforms. Guided by an esteemed leadership team with insights drawn from the world’s digital and data centre titans, Apto brings over a century of combined experience in designing, constructing and operating data centres at scale. From Day 1, the leadership team has focused on the type of company we want to build. We measure success by how well we demonstrate our values every day. We nurture an unfaltering passion for excellence, guided by our customers’ needs and expectations. We obsessively take ownership, deliver on our promises, and create lasting positive impressions. We thrive on overcoming any hurdle to give our customers the best possible experience and outcomes. And, we have fun along the way. Our access to capital and real estate is important but our most valuable asset is our team, and we are delighted now to be recruiting for a Graduate Quantity Surveyor. The role * Support the procurement of Design, General Contractor, Owner Furnished Contractor Installed (OFCI) and third party consultant work packages. * Support the development and validation of construction estimates at various stages of design. * Maintain accurate cost databases and historical cost data. * Monitor cost performance metrics and support monthly cost reporting. * Support the construction team with project close out and final accounts. * Support the management of external consultants. * Collaborate with cross functional teams to ensure effective communication and implementation of commercial processes and procedures. **Basic Qualifications** * Degree in Quantity Surveying or equivalent qualification. Location St Stephen’s Green, Dublin 2, Ireland. Office based at least three days per week. 20% travel across Europe. Employee Benefits * Competitive Salary * Eligible for bonus and Long Term Incentive Plan (LTIP). * 25 days annual leave plus your birthday and bank holidays * Company Pension Contribution * Fully supported and funded professional membership. * Comprehensive Health Insurance for you and your dependents. Life insurance, Critical Illness Cover and Income Protection Reporting Commercial Director **Apto is an equal opportunities employer.** \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Contact Apto today If you are interested, please apply with your CV! \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

title: Graduate Software Engineer
description: **About UniDoodle** UniDoodle is an Irish EdTech company founded at Maynooth University, pioneering the use of artificial intelligence in education. Our platform uniquely combines AI\-powered grading with handwriting recognition technology \- enabling educators to assess written student responses, not just typed answers. Unlike standard classroom engagement tools, UniDoodle is purpose\-built for exam preparation and academic assessment, serving a specialised and growing market across Ireland and internationally. **The Role** We are seeking a Graduate Software Engineer to join our engineering team at our Dublin office. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated recent graduate to contribute to cutting\-edge AI and EdTech development, working on technology that is genuinely changing how students learn and how educators teach. **Key Responsibilities** * Contribute to the development of UniDoodle's AI\-powered platform, including handwriting recognition and automated grading features * Write clean, efficient, and well\-documented code across the full stack * Collaborate with product and design teams to deliver meaningful new features * Participate in code reviews and uphold engineering best practices * Identify and resolve bugs and performance issues in a timely manner **Requirements** * A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related discipline * Solid understanding of web technologies and at least one backend programming language * Interest in AI, machine learning, or EdTech is a strong advantage * Strong problem\-solving skills and attention to detail * Excellent communication and teamwork abilities * A proactive attitude and willingness to take ownership **What We Offer** * A competitive graduate salary (€28,000 – €38,000\) * The opportunity to work on genuinely innovative AI technology with real educational impact * A collaborative, inclusive team culture * Professional development and mentorship from day one * Dublin city\-based role with flexible working arrangements Pay: €538\.00\-€730\.00 per week Work Location: In person

title: HGV Class 1 Driver
description: Are you searching for a long\-term job? Are you hoping to build a career? Driving for Jenkins is an opportunity like none other: We offer permanent and long\-term stability for our drivers, as well as flexibility, to provide our drivers with a work\-life balance. Jenkins Drivers are highly valued members of the business as specialists in what we do and professional representatives of the Jenkins brand as the face of our business transactions. “We at Jenkins are very much aware of the value of our Driver colleagues. We offer long\-term job security in today’s ever\-changing and challenging Transport market. We are leaders in the service we deliver and want to continue to grow this model by investing in, and developing a driving team that are trained, skilled and knowledgeable about our specialist market.” ***\-Paul Harden, Head of Transport*** **What the role does and doesn’t look like…** \-Pre\-loaded trailers \-Most loads offloaded by FLTs \-Non multi\-drop \-Curtain\-sider work \-Allocated driver to vehicle \-Non\-hazardous goods \-Non\-temperature controlled vehicles \-No cage work \-No physically demanding work \-No tail lifts \-No high street offloading \-All offloading/onloading environments set up for Class 1 vehicles \-Newly qualified drivers with no experience are welcome to apply and will be provided full inductions. **Pay Rates** €18\.14 per hour €15 subsistence per day (subject to statutory rules) *Typically our drivers earn in excess of 43k each year* **Don’t just take our word for it, ask our drivers:** “I like the family atmosphere at Jenkins. They are really fair employers\- they listen to my side of the story, I like the way the company is run. I drive a 20\-mile trip to work every day, I’d never travelled that far for work before but it’s worth it. All the kit and equipment is nice. No one else drives my truck\- that means a lot to me. The work’s clean, it’s a nice clean job, and 90% of the loading is indoors. The customer relations are really good, Jenkins always go for quality over quantity. I like it when you go to a customer and they say “Oh thank god it’s Jenkins bringing it\- it’ll be on time.” That’s down to good planning and the work the planning team do. It’s a really good working environment, everything about the job is good; lorries, trailers, people\- it really makes a difference. It’s a great team, I really can’t fault them, we all pitch in. It’s a really pleasant place to work, I get on with everybody. I’ve been here 7 years, it’s the longest I’ve been with any company and I’ve no intention of going anywhere. I still love driving because I’ve got a good job.” ***\-Jenkins Driver*** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €43,000\.00\-€47,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Licence/Certification: * C\+E Class 1 Driving licence (required) * CPC qualification (required) Work Location: In person

title: HR Graduate Aministrator Employee Benefits - German speaker
description: At MiniMed, you can begin a lifelong career of exploration and innovation, while helping make a difference in the lives of people living with diabetes around the globe. You'll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation for a more connected, compassionate world. **About The Role** This is an exciting time to join MiniMed as our teams continue to expand and we experience growth. Our remote team in the UK have an exciting opportunity for an HR graduate to join us in Ireland, as the team continue to provide a world class service in our different regions. If you are a dedicated, individual who thrives on building relationships and providing excellent customer service and working at pace with our employees, this is an opportunity worth exploring. **Responsibilities may include the following and other duties may be assigned:** * Serve as the primary point of contact for employees, managers, and HR partners regarding local benefits, leave eligibility, processes, and timelines. * Respond in a timely manner to compensation and benefits related queries through our HRIS system, Workday. * Administer and manage all local benefit and employee leave programs, including but not limited to medical, retirement, family care leave, medical leave, parental leave, and disability\-related absences. * Coordinate with third\-party leave administrators and insurance carriers to ensure accurate case management, documentation, and benefit continuation. Ensure all employees are enrolled in relevant employee benefits, help provide data for audits. * Proactively support absence management (long term illness, maternity, paternity) with HR partners to ensure return to work processes are followed and HRIS system is updated. * Have an awareness of local regulations and defined company policies. * Ensure accurate continuation, billing, and reconciliation and payment of benefits and payment to benefits providers. * Closely partner with payroll to ensure correct pay, deductions, and adjustments for benefits and leave. * Support defining system requirements that facilitate efficiency, automation, and self\-service for various HR processes. **Required Knowledge And Experience** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources or Business. * Experience in HR, compensation, or benefits administration is preferred * Experience working with HRIS (Workday) is advantageous * Biligual \- fluent German preferred **Skills And Competencies** * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple cases simultaneously * Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a customer\-focused mindset. * Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and confidentiality. * Analytical, agile and adaptable with good problem\-solving skills. * Ability to interpret policies and regulations and apply them consistently. * Comfortable working independently while collaborating with cross\-functional teams. \#BetterDaysStartNow **Physical Job Requirements** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this position, but they are not an exhaustive list of all the required responsibilities and skills of this position. **Benefits \& Compensation** **MiniMed offers a competitive salary and flexible benefits package** At MiniMed, we put people first. A commitment to our employees lives at the core of our values: We recognize their contributions. They share in the success they help create. We offer a wide range of benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans designed to support you at every stage of your career and life. **About MiniMed** We want to make every day a better day for people living with diabetes. Our team of creative innovators around the globe share a passion for finding the simplest solutions to the problems that people with diabetes face on a daily basis. For more than 40 years, we've been redefining what's possible, from intelligent dosing systems designed for real life to predictive insights that stay a step ahead, and we're dedicated to continuing to support our customers through every step of their journey — meeting them where and how they need it.

title: Handler Dublin
description: We have an exciting opportunity for you to join our Operations team as a package handler. **Who We Are** FedEx is the largest express transportation company in the world, connecting more than 220 countries and territories. We work every moment to deliver the most important package of someone’s day. Our team of passionate people know their day\-to\-day work is invaluable in delivering outstanding experience to our customers. 530,000 team members strong, we are as diverse as the world we serve. We love what we do, and we do it well. Our reach is big, and so are our dreams. Join us and let's write our next chapter together. **Working hours** : 39 per week **What You Will Be Doing** * Sorting, scanning, loading, and unloading packages in a fast\-paced environment * Using relevant equipment and technology when needed * Following our Health and Safety regulations * Supporting your supervisor and team members as required **What Do You Bring With You** * Be a great team player * Be able to complete all required training * Be able to communicate effectively, in a fast\-moving environment * Have excellent eye for detail and be able to make quick, accurate decisions **What Do We Offer** * Attractive compensation package * Training to get you started and on\-the\-job learning opportunities * Extensive learning resources to further develop your skills and knowledge * Tuition Assistance Program (\*applicable for FedEx positions with a permanent contract) * Employee Assistance Program for you and your family in difficult life situations * Employee reduced\-rate shipping * Great career opportunities * FedEx is one of the worlds most admired companies and trusted brands year after year Bring your ideas, individuality, and dreams to our global community. Feel good about where you work. Choose your career path \- we’re ready to invest in your development. Join FedEx. Diversity \& Inclusion is more than the workplace. It’s the cultures we celebrate, the dreams we inspire, and creating a global community where everyone is accepted. At FedEx, diversity and inclusion happens because we do it together. FedEx in the UK is Disability Confident ‘Committed’. FedEx was built on a philosophy that puts people first, one we take seriously. We are an equal opportunity employer and we are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce in which we provide growth opportunities for all Our Company FedEx is one of the world's largest express transportation companies and has consistently been selected as one of the top 10 World’s Most Admired Companies by "Fortune" magazine. Every day FedEx delivers for its customers with transportation and business solutions, serving more than 220 countries and territories around the globe. We can serve this global network due to our outstanding team of FedEx team members, who are tasked with making every FedEx experience outstanding. Our Philosophy The People\-Service\-Profit philosophy (P\-S\-P) describes the principles that govern every FedEx decision, policy or activity. FedEx takes care of our people; they, in turn, deliver the impeccable service demanded by our customers, who reward us with the profitability necessary to secure our future. The essential element in making the People\-Service\-Profit philosophy such a positive force for the company is where we close the circle, and return these profits back into the business, and invest back in our people. Our success in the industry is attributed to our people. Through our P\-S\-P philosophy, we have a work environment that encourages team members to be innovative in delivering the highest possible quality of service to our customers. We care for their well\-being, and value their contributions to the company. Our Culture Our culture is important for many reasons, and we intentionally bring it to life through our behaviors, actions and activities in every part of the world. The FedEx culture and values have been a cornerstone of our success and growth since we began in the early 1970’s. While other companies can copy our systems, infrastructure and processes, our culture makes us unique and is often a differentiating factor as we compete and grow in today’s global marketplace.

title: Healthcare Assistant – Bray
description: Job Title: Health Care Assistant Reports To: Health Care Supervisor / Branch Manager Location: Bray Job Type: Full\-Time and Part\-Time InisCare was established with the core principle that contented caregivers lead to satisfied customers. We maintain the belief that exceptional, dependable, and personable home care services are delivered by individuals who receive support from a skilled and empathetic leadership team. Our goal is to support our team of caregivers in delivering compassionate and professional care that enables individuals to maintain active and fulfilling lifestyles. **Job Summary** The role of a Health Care Assistant is to support the Customer to live happily and safely at home. This may involve assisting them with personal care, such as showering or bathing, assisting with dressing, assisting with incontinence care, assisting with meal preparation and light housekeeping. **Responsibilities** * Practice and maintain the most recent protocol in relation to safety procedures, such as Person Moving and Handling * Report any concerns in relation to the Customer, or colleagues if they are not working within safe boundaries to your manager * Report any safeguarding incidents or what you may think is safeguarding to your manager * Document accurately and factually in the daily logs, all tasks completed with the Customer for each shift * Always maintain confidentiality * Attend any training scheduled for you by InisCare \& Work within safe and professional boundaries * Ensure each task performed with the Customer (medication prompt, personal care, meal preparation, etc) is completed with dignity and respect for the Customer. * Encourage autonomy and decision making * Talk with the Customer throughout the shift, regardless of their verbal and cognitive ability, ensuring that all conversations are within a professional context * Attend shifts as scheduled and contact the office in a timely manner if you are unable to attend a shift or are late. Do not phone the customer directly. **Qualifications** * QQI Level 5 Health care Award or equivalent **Additional Training Requirements** * Children’s First – HSE LAND * Safeguarding – HSE LAND * Dementia Training – HSE LAND * GDPR Training – HSE LAND * AMRIC Basics of Infection Prevention and Control – HSE LAND * AMRIC Hand Hygiene – HSE LAND * Putting on and taking off PPE in Community Healthcare\- HSE LAND **Skills** * Basic patient care: bathing, dressing, feeding * Assisting with mobility and exercise * Effective verbal and written communication \& Active listening * Following instructions and protocols * Providing companionship and emotional support * Familiarity with healthcare equipment and devices * Time management and task prioritization **Competencies** * Empathy and compassion * Understanding and responding to emotional needs * Effective teamwork within a healthcare team * Supporting colleagues and positive team environment * Adaptability to changing needs and environments * Willingness to learn new skills and procedures * Attention to detail in observing and reporting * Physical stamina for demanding tasks * Reliability, punctuality and Professional appearance and demeanour * Ethical standards and confidentiality adherence * Problem\-solving and critical thinking **Experience** * One\-year paid experience or prior experience is not required if you have your QQI Level 5 **Benefits** * Competitive hourly rate from €14\.50 up to €20\.00 (depending on experience and qualifications) * Mileage * Refer\-a\-Friend Program – €150\.00 per referral * bike\-to\-work scheme * Mentor program * Sponsored QQI Level 5 (Care Skills and Care for the Elderly Person Modules) * Career Progression Plan and guidance * Welcoming Bonus €200\.00 **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. Our company is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment where all employees feel valued and respected. We do not discriminate on the basis of gender, age, race, religion, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, family status, or membership of the Traveller community. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates and are dedicated to promoting equality and preventing discrimination in our workplace. In line with the Employment Equality Acts 1998\-2015, we ensure that our recruitment, selection, training, and promotion processes are based solely on the abilities and merits of the candidates and employees. We strive to provide a work environment free from harassment and discrimination, and we support equal opportunities in all aspects of employment.

title: Hydrogeologist
description: **SLR is a global leader in sustainability solutions, working with clients to help make sustainability happen, within a collaborative, people\-first culture.** Due to continued growth, we are seeking an experienced **Senior / Associate Hydraulic \& Hydrogeology/Hydrology Consultant** (or candidates with 5\+ years’ experience) to join our established **Hydrogeology \& Hydrology team** in Ireland, delivering high\-profile water environment projects. Based in **Dublin (hybrid)** – with flexibility to be based in other Irish offices or remotely for the right candidate – the role will focus primarily on **hydrogeology and hydrology assessments and project delivery** , with additional exposure to **flood risk, drainage, and wider water environment disciplines** , as well as projects spanning renewables, infrastructure, power, industrial and built environment sectors. **The Role** **Role** You’ll support and lead the delivery of water environment projects from early planning through to detailed reporting, working closely with clients and multidisciplinary teams. The role includes: * Project and technical delivery across hydrogeology and hydrology projects * Preparation of Environmental Impact Assessment Reports (EIAR) – Water chapters * Management of water monitoring programmes and associated data analysis * Development of hydrogeological and hydrological Conceptual Site Models * Permitting and licensing support (including Discharge Licences and Industrial Emissions Licences) * Site work including supervision of drilling, groundwater monitoring and water sampling * Technical report writing and peer review * Client interaction and support with proposals and project delivery * Mentoring and supporting junior team members * A mix of desk\-based work and site visits across Ireland, with occasional travel You’ll collaborate with colleagues across Environmental and Earth Sciences, Planning, ESIA and Engineering, as well as SLR’s wider Water Environment group, including flood risk and drainage specialists, geomorphologists and geochemists. **Rewards \& Benefits** SLR’s ‘One Team’ culture is at the heart of our business, providing a collaborative and supportive environment for professional development. Through a structured career framework, regular performance reviews, and full support toward chartership, professional qualifications and licenses, we offer outstanding opportunities for long‑term career progression. **Our Flexible Benefits \& Reward Package Includes** * Competitive salary with discretionary bonus * Company pension * Private medical insurance * Life, income, and travel insurance * Salary sacrifice car lease and cycle‑to‑work schemes *We believe sustainable careers need balance, that’s why we offer:* * Flexible, hybrid and agile working * 25 days’ annual leave plus flexible public holidays * Buy or sell leave options * Core working hours * Cross‑border remote working and sabbatical opportunities **Family Life And Wellbeing Matter At SLR** We provide enhanced maternity, paternity and adoption leave, access to an Employee Assistance Programme, and specialist support for mental health, stress, and menopause \- supporting our people both professionally and personally. **About You** You’ll bring: * Solid to substantial experience in hydrogeology and/or hydrology, ideally within a consultancy or similar commercial environment * A degree in Geoscience, Hydrogeology, Hydrology, or a related discipline (postgraduate qualification advantageous) * Experience delivering EIAR Water chapters or equivalent environmental reporting * Experience in developing conceptual site models and managing field programmes * Knowledge of water permitting/licensing processes * Strong technical report writing and problem\-solving skills * Experience with GIS software (desirable) * Project management and stakeholder engagement experience * A full, clean driving licence (required for site work) **Culture \& Inclusion** Inclusion is fundamental to who we are. We are committed to equal opportunities and foster a workplace where everyone can thrive, supported by active Culture \& Inclusion Committees and Employee Networks. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and are a Disability Confident Committed employer. If you require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know and we will be happy to support you. If this role feels like a great fit for your experience and ambitions, we’d love to hear from you.

title: IT Infrastructure Engineer
description: **Job Description** **Job Title:** IT Infrastructure Engineer **Department:** IT, Infrastructure Engineering Team **Reports to:** IT Manager **Date:** 2026 **Location:** Beacon Hospital **Overall Purpose of the Job** The IT Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for supporting, maintaining, improving, and delivering enterprise infrastructure services across the hospital environment. The role involves a combination of operational support, IT infrastructure project delivery, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement across networking, virtualisation, systems, security, storage, backup, and cloud\-integrated services. The IT Infrastructure Engineer works closely with internal IT teams, including Cyber Security, Helpdesk, and Development teams, as well as clinical departments, vendors, and third\-party providers, to ensure stable, secure, and resilient infrastructure services within a complex healthcare environment supporting 24x7x365 operations. The successful candidate will be a strong IT infrastructure generalist with solid technical fundamentals, strong troubleshooting abilities, and practical experience across enterprise infrastructure technologies, with deeper technical capability in one or more core areas such as networking, virtualisation, systems administration, storage, or security technologies. Engineers are encouraged to develop broad infrastructure knowledge while building deeper technical expertise in one or more infrastructure domains. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables** **Infrastructure Operations \& Engineering** * Support, maintain, troubleshoot, and improve enterprise infrastructure systems and services across the hospital environment. * Investigate and resolve infrastructure incidents, operational issues, and escalated technical support requests in a timely manner. * Take ownership of infrastructure issues, operational improvements, and assigned technical projects from investigation through to resolution and delivery. * Support and improve enterprise infrastructure platforms, including networking, virtualisation, storage, backup, Microsoft infrastructure, and cloud\-integrated services. * Maintain and troubleshoot enterprise networking environments, including switching, routing, VLANs, VPN connectivity, wireless infrastructure, load balancing, and firewall technologies. * Support virtualisation and server platforms, including Windows, Linux, physical servers, and virtual infrastructure technologies. * Support enterprise storage and backup platforms and participate in backup validation, recovery testing, and disaster recovery activities. * Support identity and infrastructure services, including Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, Microsoft 365, and related infrastructure technologies. * Support infrastructure connectivity and integrations for healthcare and clinical systems. * Support enterprise monitoring, alerting, reporting, and operational stability initiatives. * Maintain technical documentation, operational procedures, implementation notes, and infrastructure diagrams. **Security \& Resilience** * Assist with implementing infrastructure security improvements, including hardening, segmentation, patching, resilience, and access control initiatives. * Support secure infrastructure operations through firewall administration, certificate management, and security best practices. * Participate in resilience and recoverability initiatives across the hospital infrastructure environment. **Projects \& Continuous Improvement** * Participate in infrastructure projects, including upgrades, migrations, expansions, implementations, and service improvements. * Work collaboratively with vendors and third\-party providers during implementations, upgrades, troubleshooting, and maintenance activities. * Contribute to infrastructure standardisation, operational improvements, and continuous service enhancement initiatives. * Work collaboratively across IT teams to ensure stable, secure, and efficient infrastructure operations. * Participate in planned maintenance activities and out\-of\-hours support where required. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Infrastructure, Networking, or related discipline. * Minimum 4\-5 years' experience in enterprise infrastructure, systems, network, or infrastructure engineering environments. * Experience supporting enterprise infrastructure technologies, including networking, virtualisation, Microsoft infrastructure, storage, backup, or security platforms. * Practical experience in one or more core infrastructure areas such as networking, virtualisation, systems administration, storage, or security technologies. * Experience in troubleshooting complex infrastructure and operational issues within enterprise environments. * Experience participating in infrastructure projects, upgrades, migrations, or technology implementations. * Experience working with vendors and third\-party providers during support and project delivery activities. * Healthcare infrastructure experience is desirable but not essential. **Preferred Certifications \& Technical Exposure** * Relevant infrastructure certifications are desirable, particularly in Microsoft, virtualisation, enterprise networking, firewall, cloud, or cybersecurity technologies. * Exposure to enterprise networking, firewall, virtualisation, storage, backup, Microsoft infrastructure, and cloud\-integrated technologies is desirable. * Experience with enterprise healthcare systems and integrations would be advantageous. **Technical \& Operational Competencies** * Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem\-solving skills within enterprise infrastructure environments. * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced operational healthcare environment supporting critical systems and services. * Ability to manage multiple priorities, operational tasks, and infrastructure projects simultaneously. * Strong communication, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement skills. * Ability to work independently while contributing positively within a team environment. * Strong documentation and organisational skills. * Customer\-focused approach with a strong service delivery mindset. * Willingness to continuously develop technical knowledge and broader infrastructure capabilities. * Ability to participate in planned maintenance activities, on\-call support, and out\-of\-hours operational work where required. **Additional Information** All posts within Beacon Hospital require flexibility to support the delivery of effective and efficient healthcare services. The post holder will be expected to support operational requirements, infrastructure projects, cybersecurity remediation initiatives, and continuous service improvement activities as required by hospital management and the IT department. This job description is intended to outline the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be reviewed in line with changing operational and business requirements.

title: IT Ops Procurement Technician L1
description: **Company Overview** -------------------- Milestone Technologies is a global IT managed services firm that partners with organizations to scale their technology, infrastructure and services to drive specific business outcomes such as digital transformation, innovation, and operational agility. Milestone is focused on building an employee\-first, performance\-based culture and for over 25 years, we have a demonstrated history of supporting category\-defining enterprise clients that are growing ahead of the market. Our seasoned professionals deliver services based on Milestone’s best practices and service delivery framework. By leveraging our vast knowledge base to execute initiatives, we deliver both short\-term and long\-term value to our clients and apply continuous service improvement to deliver transformational benefits to IT. With Intelligent Automation, Milestone helps businesses further accelerate their IT transformation. The result is a sharper focus on business objectives and a dramatic improvement in employee productivity. Through our key technology partnerships and our people\-first approach, Milestone continues to deliver industry\-leading innovation to our clients. With more than 3,000 employees serving over 200 companies worldwide, we are following our mission of revolutionizing the way IT is deployed. **Job Overview** ---------------- ### **Service Desk Technician L2 with A/V Events experience** **(Predominantly MAC/iOS Environment \- 90%)** ### ### **Location****:** **Dublin, Ireland \- Monday through Friday** We’re looking for a Service Desk Technician to join our skilled IT Help Desk Operations team, serving local and global internal users as well as A/V Events. The Help Desk Technician has experience in supporting both Mac and PC environments, can hold a conversation as well as resolve complex technical issues effectively and efficiently, and is someone who values the people behind the problems. and takes actions based on the current trends in Service.** * ### **Document our systems and ensure our processes are always improving.** * ### **Displays learning agility by actively seeking answers when technically challenged.** * ### **Seeks feedback from other Help Desk Technicians to optimize and improve support, while also maintaining a solid understanding of general user support needs and requirements.** \#LI\-NB2**Compensation** ---------------- Estimated Pay Range: Exact compensation and offers of employment are dependent on circumstances of each case and will be determined based on job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, licenses or certifications, and location. **Our Commitment to Diversity \& Inclusion** -------------------------------------------- *At Milestone we strive to create a workplace that reflects the communities we serve and work with, where we all feel empowered to bring our full, authentic selves to work. We know creating a diverse and inclusive culture that champions equity and belonging is not only the right thing to do for our employees but is also critical to our continued success.* *Milestone Technologies provides equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, marital status, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, national origin, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law, or local ordinance. Milestone also makes reasonable accommodations for disabled applicants and employees.* *We welcome the unique background, culture, experiences, knowledge, innovation, self\-expression and perspectives you can bring to our global community. Our recruitment team is looking forward to meeting you.*

title: IT Support Engineer L1/L2 - MSP
description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in South County Dublin is hiring a Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around 3\-4\+ years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment. MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a 5 days per week office\-based role. This is non\-negotiable. You must already be living in South County Dublin, and you must be fully legal to work in Ireland now. There is no visa sponsorship and no relocation support for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **4\+ years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Salary €35,000 \- €39,000\. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: Infrastructure Engineer
description: Crusoe is on a mission *to accelerate the abundance of energy and intelligence* . As the only vertically integrated AI infrastructure company built from the ground up, we own and operate each layer of the stack — from electrons to tokens — to power the world's most ambitious AI workloads. When you join Crusoe, you join a team that is building the future, faster. We're in the midst of the greatest industrial revolution of our time. The demand for AI compute is boundless, and power is a bottleneck. We're solving that — with an energy\-first approach that makes AI infrastructure better for the world and faster for the people innovating with AI. We're looking for problem\-solving, opportunity\-finding teammates with a sense of urgency, who believe in the scale of our ambition and thrive on a path not fully paved — people who want to grow their careers alongside a team of experts across energy, manufacturing, data center construction, and cloud services. If you want to do the most meaningful work of your career, help our customers and partners advance their AI strategies, and be part of a high\-performing team that believes in each other, come build with us at Crusoe. **About the Role:** At Crusoe, the Infrastructure Engineers on our Fleet Operations team play a crucial role in ensuring the reliability and stability of our hardware platform. This role involves both hands\-on diagnosis and repair of rack\-level GPU hardware, as well as developing automation to streamline fleet management, capacity delivery, and maintenance operations. The ideal candidate will be working closely with Data Center Operations and Engineering teams, and playing a key part in the continuous improvement of our hardware platform's reliability and scalability, ensuring that our cutting\-edge infrastructure, featuring the latest NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, continue to operate at peak efficiency for our customers. **What You’ll Be Working On** * Problem Solving and Deep\-Level Troubleshooting: Investigating and troubleshooting problems and hardware faults that our automation can’t determine within our GPU platforms. This will involve taking data from system logs, kernel logs, BMC redfish APIs, and if the data is not there, working with hardware and kernel engineers to add information you need to make accurate determinations. * Coordination and Collaboration: Working closely with our Data Centre Operations, Hardware Engineering and Capacity Planning teams to repair and remediate failed hardware, ensure consistent delivery of new hardware to customers, and roll out new upgrades across the fleet * Automation and Tool Development: Automate routine processes and build Crusoe’s hardware diagnostics, provisioning and repair tooling * Build Processes and Documentation: When you figure out the best way to do something, you’ll be working on building processes, documentation and tooling to help the next person who finds this problem * Validate and Test new hardware: Crusoe is often the first company in the world to get the latest generation AI hardware, before it’s fully tested. Conducting rigorous testing and validation on such cutting\-edge hardware and servers that comes back from repair * On\-Call: Participate in our on\-call rotation, partnering with our US teams to provide follow\-the\-sun coverage **What You’ll Bring To The Team** * Strong analytical, troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills: Our automation takes care of the easy problems, you’ll be digging deep to figure out the hard ones * Linux experience: You’ll have solid unAbout the Rolederstanding of Linux internals and feel at home working in a terminal * Server Hardware and Provisioning: Exposure to server\-class hardware \& provisioning * Fundamentals of Hardware and Networking: You don’t need to be an expert, but you should know if an error message is due to a failed hardware component, a firmware bug, or a networking misconfiguration without escalating * Excellent communication and collaboration skills: You’ll be working with many different people across a lot of different teams – communication is critical * Education: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, related field, or self\-educated in computer science fundamentals. **Bonus Points** * Large\-scale GPU operations: We work with cutting edge hardware and software, so we understand most people won’t have worked with it – but it would be nice if you have! * Programming Proficiency: Proficiency with at least one programming language (Python, Go, or similar). **Benefits:** Crusoe also offers a competitive benefits package designed to support financial security, health, and overall well\-being, including pension contributions, private health and dental insurance, income protection, life assurance, and more. **Compensation:** Compensation will be paid as a salary or hourly. Compensation to be determined by the applicant’s education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data. Crusoe is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, citizenship, marital status, sex/gender, sexual preference/ orientation, gender identity, age, veteran status, national origin, or any other status protected by law or regulation.
