title: Intern, Student Operations
description: **ABOUT GREYSTAR** Greystar is a leading, fully integrated global real estate platform offering expertise in property management, investment management, development, and construction services in institutional\-quality rental housing. Headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina, Greystar manages and operates over $300 billion of real estate in more than 265 markets globally with offices throughout North America, Europe, South America, and the Asia\-Pacific region. Greystar is the largest operator of apartments in the United States, managing over one million units/beds globally. Across its platforms, Greystar has nearly $79 billion of assets under management, including over $35 billion of development assets and over $36\.5 billion of regulatory assets under management. Greystar was founded by Bob Faith in 1993 to become a provider of world\-class service in the rental residential real estate business. To learn more, visit www.greystar.com. **JOB DESCRIPTION SUMMARY** As a Student Operations Intern, you will support the full lifecycle of a Greystar student community. This role blends on\-site resident hospitality with operational and commercial management, focusing on the unique needs of the student demographic and the critical Summer period. Please note that this is a 10\-week internship during June and August.**JOB DESCRIPTION** ### **Key Responsibilities** * **Full Lifecycle Support:** Own the administrative journey from initial enquiry and room viewings to contract signing, guarantor verification, and the high\-intensity "Summer Turn" periods. * **Front\-of\-House Query Management:** Act as the first point of contact for all resident enquiries, resolving issues ranging from parcel collection and maintenance requests to account queries with speed and empathy. * **Leasing \& Compliance Admin:** Maintain accurate resident records within property management systems ensuring all tenancy documentation and right\-to\-rent checks meet compliance standards. * **Summer Short\-Stay Operations:** Support the transition to short\-term tenancies during the summer months, managing high\-frequency check\-ins for interns and summer school groups to maximize asset occupancy. * **Head Office Operational Support:** Bridge the gap between site and Head Office by assisting the Operations team with portfolio\-wide data entry, occupancy reporting, and process improvement projects. * **Operational \& Financial Admin:** Support lease processing, guarantor verification, and rent collection. Gain exposure to reporting, financial and compliance standards (Maintenance/Facilities/Health \& Safety) to ensure asset protection. ### **About You** * **The Student Expert:** Currently enrolled in a degree (Real Estate, Hospitality, or Business) with an interest in the Living sector. * **Agile Communicator:** Comfortable pivoting from supporting a student to presenting data to an Operations Manager * **Tech\-Fluent:** Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and ready to learn specialised platforms like Yardi or Entrata ***Important Notice:*** *Greystar will never request your banking details or other sensitive personal information during the interview process. Greystar does not conduct any interviews via text or messaging, and all communication will come from official Greystar email addresses (@greystar.com). If you receive suspicious requests, please report them immediately to AskHR@greystar.com.*

title: Internal Auditor
description: Due to the continued expansion of the Group, an opportunity has arisen within the Internal Audit function. The successful candidate will work closely with the Head of Internal Audit to assist in the delivery of the agreed audit schedule included in the annual audit plan. The role will involve some travel globally ( circa 20%) and offers a hybrid working arrangement. **Responsibilities** * Planning, executing and completing reviews of financial and operational processes in line with the Internal Audit Plan. * Producing detailed working papers and drafting clear, concise reports and obtaining management responses prior to their finalisation. * Offering insights, expertise, and advice and constructively challenging current controls. * Ensuring assignments are delivered in defined time scales. * Working with the business to ensure that issues raised are addressed and recommendations implemented in a timely manner. * Performing follow\-up audits to ensure appropriate action plans raised in both internal and external audit reports have been implemented and are operating effectively. * Assisting in the preparation of Audit Committee and Senior Management communications. * Working closely with Head of Internal Audit and other Heads of business units, including Finance, Risk and Legal to improve corporate governance, risk management and internal controls. * Building strong professional relationships with the leadership in the business. * Encouraging the practice of strong internal controls across the group. * Liaising with external auditors as required. **Qualifications** * ACA Qualified, Big 4 trained with 0 \- least 2 years PQE. * Strong process, technical and analytical skills, combined with strong commercial acumen. * Ability to challenge management constructively on established practices and policies where necessary. * Ability to work on own initiative while also being a good team player. * Working knowledge of Microsoft Office programs, specifically, Excel, Word, Outlook and PowerPoint * Experience using Power BI/Analytical tools would also be an advantage.

title: Investment Analyst
description: ***On behalf of an established Irish real estate investment and asset management business in Dublin, we are looking to hire an Investment Analyst.*** This is a fantastic opportunity for an ambitious, technically strong analyst to join a high\-quality team and develop their career at the heart of a growing real estate investment business. You will work closely with and learn directly from senior leadership across investment appraisal, portfolio analysis and day\-to\-day asset management. **The Role:** * Support financial modelling and investment appraisal on acquisitions, disposals and portfolio transactions * Assist with the analysis of asset performance, yields, returns and capital allocation across the portfolio * Prepare investment papers and presentations for board and senior stakeholders * Monitor market trends, comparable transactions and macro factors relevant to the sector * Work closely with asset management, finance and external advisors on live transactions **The Person:** * 1\-3 years of relevant experience in real estate investment, corporate finance, investment banking or a related field, including strong candidates from bank graduate programmes or property agencies * Strong financial modelling skills; DCF, IRR and investment appraisal experience is desirable * Real estate experience is preferred, though strong candidates from adjacent backgrounds will be considered * Currently pursuing or on a clear pathway to a professional qualification (ACA, CFA Chartered Surveyor, or similar) advantageous but not essential * Sharp analytical mind with strong attention to detail and good communication skills This is an excellent opportunity for an up\-and\-coming finance professional to take a meaningful step forward in their career within a well\-regarded real estateplatform. For more information, apply within or contact Conor Dignam at cdignam@alliedtalent.ie

title: Investment Associate
description: We are partnering with a leading European private equity firm on an investment associate search. This is an excellent opportunity to join a hands on investment platform, gaining exposure across the full deal lifecycle from evaluating and executing investments to supporting portfolio company growth, M\&A activity, and value creation initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Evaluate investment opportunities through market, industry, and commercial analysis. * Conduct financial modelling, valuation, and investment analysis. * Support deal execution, including due diligence, negotiations, and transaction structuring. * Partner with portfolio company management teams to drive growth and strategic initiatives. * Identify and assess acquisition opportunities across the portfolio. * Support value creation projects, M\&A activity, and exit processes. **Key Requirements** * 3–5 years' experience in private equity, investment banking, corporate finance, transaction services, strategy consulting, or corporate development. * Strong financial modelling, valuation, and analytical skills. * Excellent academic background. * Commercially astute with an entrepreneurial mindset. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Proactive, intellectually curious, and comfortable taking ownership. * Passion for business, investing, and value creation. * Industry or operational experience is an advantage. *Due to the volume of applications, only candidates whose experience closely matches the requirements of the role will be contacted.*

title: Italian Investment Counsellor
description: **Overview** Are you interested in working for an outstanding global investment firm and looking to take the next step in your career in Finance? Want to become an expert in and build a career advising clients on financial markets and investments? Want to focus on forging strong client relationships and providing exceptional client service? Fisher Investments Ireland is a European subsidiary of Fisher Investments, a privately\-held investment management firm that provides professional discretionary portfolio management services for high net worth private clients and some of the world's preeminent institutions. Our organisation has grown significantly over the past decade and we are looking for motivated candidates with finance backgrounds to join our team as Investment Counsellors to help us better the financial industry in Italy as we continue our expansion in Europe. **The Opportunity:** The Investment Counsellor (IC) role is focused on managing relationships with high\-net\-worth clients in Italy from our office in Dublin. Your understanding of our clients' needs and ability to educate them on the reasoning behind portfolio changes is crucial to your success in this role. You will report to your Team Manager who will aid in your professional development. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Build long standing relationships with our clients, ensuring they always receive exceptional service * Manage a book of high\-net\-worth relationships with approximately €100\-150 million in assets * Be the conduit between our clients, our Investment Policy Committee, our research and trading departments, and several service teams * Proactively update clients on portfolio changes, financial markets, our current market strategy and investment philosophy * Gain advanced finance and capital markets education through our training program **Qualifications:** * 2\+ years exposure to private clients within the banking /wealth management industry * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, current events and the market * Native Italian and native or fluent English language skills (Proficiency in other European languages is beneficial) * Connect with and educate a variety of audiences * Maintain a positive personality and empathise with clients in good times and bad * A Bachelor's Degree, Master Degree or CFA is beneficial * QFA or CISI level 4 is beneficial **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Junior Associate - Employment
description: **The opportunity** We are looking for a junior Associate to join our growing team in Dublin. You would be working with our Employment Team and International Employment Group on a mix of contentious, noncontentious and corporate support work. **The team** Working with large and small corporations across all industry\-sectors impacted by technology, we offer clients a comprehensive range of legal advice across the full spectrum of employment law. Our expertise includes: Crossborder projects; Workforce restructuring and reductions; Employment support on corporate transactions; Senior level appointments and terminations; Complex disputes; Representation and resolution of claims before the Workplace Relations Commission and Irish courts; Outsourcing; Benefits and incentives; Performance management; Workplace investigations; Global mobility; Policies, procedures and in\-house documentation; and Protection of confidential information. The new team in Dublin consists of one partner and one senior associate and your hire is a key step in our growth strategy as we scale up to meet client demand. The team sits within a growing Dublin office that is expanding since its opened in June 2022\. The team works closely with colleagues across the International Employment Group and there are opportunities to travel to these offices. Start date \- ASAP Download

title: Junior BCAR Coordinator
description: Stewart Construction, a top Irish Contractor with over 120 years of excellence in the industry, is currently seeking a proactive and organised **Junior BCAR Coordinator** to join our growing and supportive team. You'll work alongside experienced professionals on prestigious commercial, industrial, and educational projects. This is an exciting opportunity to grow your experience with a company known for delivering high\-quality work across Ireland. You will be working within a well\-established team on a variety of prestigious projects. The current workload is exciting and you will be involved at all stages. This is a great opportunity to be part of an expanding team. As a member of this team, you will have the opportunity to develop and bring added value to your career and gain additional skills with a BIM enabled contractor. **Key Responsibilities and Duties** * Carrying out quality inspections on all works with BCAR Manager/ Senior BCAR Co\-ordinator. * Collation of all project documentation relating to the assigned certifier/design certifier's inspection plans and the Building Control Amendment Regulations 2014\. * Liaising with the Assigned Certifier and Resident Engineers on NCR’s raised and review of close observations with them. * Co\-ordination of commencement notices * Co\-ordination of inspection plans and tracking of same during the course of the projects. * Compilation of required product certification from contractors with BCAR Manager/ Senior BCAR Co\-ordinator. * Tracking of open compliance issues on projects. * Co\-ordination and liaison with all parties involved in the construction projects. * Report weekly and monthly data and information to site teams, key stakeholders and partners. * Monitor BCAR Compliance, inspection status, quality performance and outstanding actions * Support project audits, reviews and compliance assessments as required. * Promote best practice in quality management, inspection processes and regulatory compliance. * Contribute to lessons learned initiatives and continuous improvement across the business. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of Building Regulations, BCAR requirements, industry standard and emerging best practices. * Miscellaneous duties and responsibilities as may reasonably be required by the Company from time to time. **Requirements:** * Minimum of 2 years’ experience with a Main Contractor (commercial project experience desirable). * Third level construction management, engineering, architectural, or surveying background. * Knowledge of Quality Control and Building Regulations in the construction industry. * Strong team member with the ability to identify and support implementation of quality improvements * Committed to Continued Professional development, willingness to learn, \& passionate about better building. * Good communication skills \- Must have the ability to communicate effectively. * Good administrative/organisational ability with high level of attention to detail. **Desirable:** * Valid Safepass and Manual Handling certification * Full Drivers licence

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: **Junior Marketing Executive – Cabins \& Containers (Full\-Time)** **Join Our Growing Team** **Cabins \& Containers Ireland** are actively seeking a proactive, creative, and results\-driven **Junior Marketing Executive** to help grow our brand, generate leads, and expand our marketing presence across the Irish market. accommodation units, and bespoke modular solutions. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and implement marketing strategies to promote our range of cabins, containers, modular buildings, welfare units, Christina@cabinsandcontainers.ie **Cabins \& Containers – Providing Innovative Modular Solutions Across Ireland and Beyond.** Pay: €29,000\.00\-€32,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Junior Planner
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for a **Junior** **Planner** to join our growing team in Dublin. The successful Planner will be responsible for developing, maintaining, and monitoring project programmes across a range of infrastructure and utilities works, ensuring projects are delivered safely, on time, and within budget. Experience working under NEC contracts is highly desirable. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Develop, manage, and update detailed project programmes using Primavera P6 and/or MS Project Work closely with Project Managers, Engineers, and Commercial teams to produce realistic and achievable schedules Monitor project progress, identify risks and delays, and propose mitigation measures Prepare short\-term and look\-ahead programmes Carry out programme impact and delay analysis where required Support change management and compensation events (particularly under NEC contracts) Attend project and progress meetings, providing planning updates and reports Ensure programmes align with construction methodologies, procurement schedules, and resource plans Assist in the preparation of tender programmes and planning inputs for bids Maintain accurate planning records and reporting dashboards **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Degree or relevant qualification in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline 1\+ years experience as a Planner within Civils and/or Utilities projects Strong knowledge of construction sequencing and methodologies Proficiency in Primavera P6 and/or MS Project **NEC Contract Experience Is Preferred** Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills Ability to work collaboratively with multidisciplinary teams Excellent attention to detail and time management skills **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Procurement Officer
description: Junior Procurement Officer What we do at Mckeon Group McKeon Group is a long\-established Irish construction and building services contractor, delivering projects across sectors including commercial, healthcare, education and heritage. With over 70 years of experience, the Group offers construction, fit\-out and facilities services to both public and private clients. Hereworks, its technology division, specialises in smart building solutions, audio\-visual systems and full\-stack integration, enhancing user experience and operational efficiency. Together, they provide a comprehensive approach to building and technology projects, combining traditional craftsmanship with modern innovation. What’s the opportunity? We are currently looking for an ambitious, self\-motivated individual to join McKeon Group \& Hereworks as a Junior Procurement Officer. In this role, you’ll play an important part in our operations team, supporting procurement, accounts, ESG, and fleet and tool management. You’ll report directly to the Procurement Manager and work closely with both internal colleagues and external vendors, while managing documentation and procurement schedules. Benefits of joining Mckeon Group * McKeon Group \& Hereworks prides itself on its exceptional and motivated people and you will be joining a professional, warm, welcoming and enthusiastic team. * Pension Scheme * Income Protection and Death in Service scheme * Membership in Employee Assistance Programme * Excellent opportunity to advance your career and progress within the Group * Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications What will you be doing? (Responsibilities) * Procurement: Prepare and issue purchase orders using our procurement software. Build strong relationships with suppliers to ensure timely handling of enquiries. * Document Control: Support the accounts team by ensuring supplier delivery documentation is processed promptly via our procurement system. * Quotations \& Tenders: Issue enquiries to suppliers for materials, plant hire, tools and vehicles. Review quotes and prepare analysis for the Procurement Manager. * Fleet Administrator: Act as the main point of contact for managing the company fleet. * Hilti OnTrack Administrator: Oversee the management of plant and tools using Hilti OnTrack software. * ESG Support: Support the Procurement Manager with the rollout of our ESG initiatives over the next 12–24 months. Assist with implementing software used to measure and report the Group’s carbon footprint. What do you need? * Minimum of 1\-2 years of experience in an administrative role. * Previous experience in a Procurement role is highly advantageous, although experience does not need to be within the Construction or Technology sectors. * Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Proficient in the use of Microsoft Excel. * Knowledge of Construction and/or Technology, products and services is highly advantageous. * Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work well in a team and liaise effectively with various departments. * Excellent problem\-solving abilities, proactive, with a can\-do attitude. * Ability to handle sensitive information with the utmost discretion. **Attributes We Value** * Positive, service\-focused attitude * Results\-oriented approach * Excellent written and verbal communication * High attention to detail * Self\-starter who actively looks for ways to improve * Team player who’s flexible and hardworking * Honest, reliable and quick\-thinking * Strong decision\-making and problem\-solving skills * Effective time management and task prioritisation Practicalities **Salary:** DOE **Location:** Head Office (Ashbourne), client sites as required. A Hybrid working option is available after probation. McKeon Group Core Values

title: Junior Product Designer
description: **Ireland based only.** **Please note: We are not engaging recruitment agencies for this position.** **Important: This role does not meet the requirements for work permit application.** **The Product Design Team** The Product Design team is a collaborative group of designers and researchers working across multiple locations, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, Data, and Commercial teams to create player\-centred experiences at scale. The team is passionate about delivering high\-quality, data\-informed design solutions that balance user needs with business objectives. A strong focus is placed on craft, clarity of thinking, continuous improvement, and measurable impact. The team embraces modern tools and AI\-enabled workflows to work more effectively, enhance collaboration, and drive innovation across the product experience. **What you will be doing:** **Craft \& Execution** * Deliver clear, usable, and accessible design work across web and mobile platforms. * Create wireframes, flows, and high fidelity designs using Figma. * Use the Gaming design system and contribute suggestions for improvement. * Iterate on designs based on feedback, data, and research insights. **Product Thinking** * Work with Product Managers and Engineers to understand user problems and business goals. * Break down problem spaces into clear and testable solutions. * Develop an understanding of how user needs, technical constraints, and commercial goals intersect. **Collaboration \& Communication** * Collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and stakeholders throughout the design and delivery process. * Participate in critiques and team rituals, seeking feedback and contributing ideas. * Support clear documentation and handover to ensure design intent is delivered in build. **Leadership \& Influence** * Take ownership of your work by bringing clarity, asking thoughtful questions, and following through on delivery. * Demonstrate a willingness to learn from others and contribute to a supportive team environment. **Strategic Impact** * Contribute design work that supports both player satisfaction and business performance. * Develop an understanding of how your work aligns with team and company goals. **Inclusive Design \& Team Culture** * Embed accessibility and inclusive design practices into your work. * Contribute positively to team culture through collaboration, openness, and participation in team rituals. **Design Systems** * Work within the design system and follow established patterns and standards. * Suggest improvements and learn how systems evolve over time. **Customer Research** * Support research activities such as usability testing and insight gathering. * Demonstrate a natural curiosity about our players, seeking to deeply understand their needs, motivations, behaviours, and pain points. * Apply research findings to improve your design solutions, with a focus on influencing positive human behavioural change and delivering meaningful player outcomes. **Who we are looking for:** * 1 to 3 years of experience designing digital products, or equivalent experience through internships, education, or strong portfolio work. * A portfolio that demonstrates structured thinking, problem solving, and attention to detail. * Comfortable using Figma and familiar with design systems. * Strong collaboration skills and a growth mindset. * Able to manage your time across multiple tasks with guidance and support. * Interested in gaming, entertainment products, or complex consumer platforms. * Demonstrates curiosity, initiative, and a growth mindset. * Collaborates effectively with others and contributes positively to team culture. * User\-focused, detail\-oriented, and open to feedback and continuous improvement. **What we offer:** * Competitive salary * Company pension * Discretionary annual bonus depending on your performance and the company's performance * Tax Saver Commuter scheme * Bike to work scheme * Comprehensive health insurance * Income Protection Plan * Life Assurance * e\-learning platforms (plenty of resources for your development) * Referral Programme * Flexitime * 24\-day annual leave that increases with length of service * Birthday Leave (1 extra day off to celebrate your day) **More about evoke** We’re a business that embraces change and progress. The power behind big name brands William Hill, 888 and Mr Green, **evoke** is the new name for 888 Holdings. Marking a new sense of purpose, direction and ambition for the business, there couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we accelerate our journey to bring even greater delight to our customers with world\-class betting and gaming experiences. title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER
description: Exciting news! We're opening up a new location in South County Dublin later this Summer! **We’re opening applications now, but we’ll start contacting candidates from July – so if you don’t hear from us right away, we haven’t forgotten you.** We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. You know great food when you see (and taste) it. * Process Perfection – Know the menu, ingredients and procedures inside out. Follow our SOPs to keep everything efficient and consistent. * Helping Hand – Support the Kitchen Leader with stock checks, rotation, and keeping an eye on kitchen operations. * All Hands on Deck – Ready to jump in when it’s busy, support the service line and work wherever you’re needed most. ****Who You Are**** * Food Enthusiast – You’re passionate about food and get genuinely excited about fresh, seasonal ingredients. * Curious – You enjoy learning new things, ask questions, and want to get better every day. * Innovative Spirit – You love being part of a team that’s always improving and evolving. * Value\-Driven – You bring a strong work ethic and a positive attitude to every shift. * Love for Hospitality – You enjoy the buzz of a restaurant and take pride in great food and great service. * Team Player – Always ready to help your teammates and support the people around you. ****Why Work at Sprout**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities – we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team who genuinely care ****What We’re Looking For**** You don’t need to be a chef, but you do need a passion for food and a commitment to quality. Perfectly sliced carrots? No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. title: Laboratory Analyst in Forensic Science Ireland
description: **Job Title: Laboratory Analyst in Forensic Science Ireland** **Employer:** Forensic Science Ireland **County:** Celbridge, Co Kildare **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 2nd July 2026 **Starting Salary:** €42,098 **About the Employer:** Forensic Science Ireland (FSI) is an associated office of the Department of Justice, Home Affairs \& Migration and plays a pivotal role in Ireland’s criminal justice system. Its mission is to provide the Irish Justice System with a high\-quality integrated Forensic Service through independent, expert scientific analysis and advice to best international standards. FSI is based in a new purpose\-built facility at the Backweston Laboratory Campus, Celbridge, Co. Kildare. It currently has 210 staff, which includes scientists, Garda experts and analysts trained in forensic testing and reporting techniques, supported by administration and ICT professionals. FSI is expected to increase staffing and capacity in the coming years to support the growing demand for its services. Analytical staff work across three main disciplines: • Physical Analysis • Chemical Analysis and • DNA and Biological Analysis. FSI now has vacancies at entry level Laboratory Analyst. This is a great opportunity for scientific and technical professionals to become involved in a dynamic and growing professional STEM\-based organisation in a progressive field that is critical for Ireland’s criminal Justice system. **About the Role:** Reporting to a Senior Laboratory Analyst, Laboratory Analysts will be involved in delivering on a range of analytical techniques and generating reliable results on an on\-going basis. Laboratory Analysts at FSI play a pivotal role in ensuring that laboratory equipment and processes are operating to the necessary high standards for an ISO\-accredited organisation. Successful candidates will also help implement and validate new techniques to ensure that FSI is at the forefront of forensic technology and practice. The Principal Duties and Responsibilities of the Role will include some or all of the following: * Operating, calibrating and maintaining specific instruments and automated analytical equipment; * Performing routine analyses on drugs, body fluids or trace evidence; * Ensuring smooth running of the laboratory, e.g. stock control, room supplies, reagents etc; * Ensuring that all analyses are carried out in accordance with the Laboratory’s quality and safety policies; * Acquiring knowledge of underlying analytical principles and relevant legislative or other background information relevant to the task being performed; * Using the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) appropriately; * Maintaining records of analytical data and calculations and movements of exhibits and samples; * Participating in collaborative and validation studies and performing other non\- routine analyses under the supervision of scientists; * Reporting all analytical results to supervisory staff in an efficient and timely manner; and * Giving evidence in Court if required. Please note: The above is a general guide to the key duties and responsibilities of the role and is not an exhaustive description. Other duties and responsibilities appropriate to the role may also be assigned from time to time. Office Location and Vacancies Successful candidates will be based at Forensic Science Ireland, Backweston Laboratory Campus, Celbridge, Co. Kildare. Please note: Successful candidates may be placed in any of the areas of analysis/disciplines in FSI. There are currently a number of Laboratory Analyst vacancies. The Closing Date for this competition is Thursday, 2 July at 3pm. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

But getting it right requires human expertise. We're looking for professionals with legal, policy, or government knowledge to help evaluate and improve AI\-generated content in these critical areas. Your insight will directly shape how AI systems reason about Irish and European legislation, regulatory frameworks, and public policy — helping build tools that people and organisations can genuinely trust. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review AI\-generated legal analyses, policy summaries, and government\-related content for accuracy and completeness * Evaluate the proper use of legal terminology, citations, and regulatory references — including Irish statute law, EU directives, and case law * Identify factual errors, logical flaws, or misleading interpretations in AI outputs * Assess whether AI responses appropriately handle nuance across legislation, case law, compliance, and public policy * Provide clear, structured written feedback that helps improve AI performance on legal and government topics * Work independently and asynchronously — entirely on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Strong understanding of legal concepts, government processes, or public policy — Irish or EU frameworks particularly valued * Excellent reading comprehension and analytical thinking skills * Sharp attention to detail — especially around precise language and factual accuracy * Clear, structured written communication style in English * Comfortable evaluating content across diverse legal and regulatory topics * No prior AI experience required **Nice to Have** * Background in law, political science, public administration, or a related field * Experience with legal research, compliance work, or government operations in Ireland or the EU * Familiarity with GDPR, DPC enforcement, Irish data protection or financial regulation * Paralegal, legal assistant, or policy research experience The operator will also maintain accurate records and carry out additional bulk store\-related tasks as required. **Responsibilities / Principal Duties** * Operate the Company’s loading shovels and other mobile plant to load and unload bulk cargo to and from Company stores. * Prepare and clean stores between shipments and perform other operational tasks as required. * Clean, maintain, and conduct routine checks on all loading shovels and mobile plant equipment. * Maintain the cleanliness and upkeep of stores, yards, and ancillary areas. * Keep accurate records and carry out duties related to quality control, feed hygiene, health and safety, and other compliance programmes, including product temperature checks. * Operate the Company’s weighbridge software as required, supporting weighbridge operators. * Undertake any other duties reasonably required by management and support shipside operations where applicable. **Qualifications and Skills** * Full, clean Category B driving licence, or an equivalent licence valid in Ireland. * Fluent in English. * Proven experience operating large loading shovels and related machinery, including loading trailers with high\-tip buckets. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Knowledge of animal feeds, correct handling methods, storage quality preservation, and identification of potential quality issues. **Hours of Work** The normal hours of work are eight hours per day, worked on a rotating two\-shift cycle as follows: * **07:00 to 15:00** * **13:00 to 21:00** Employees will be required to work either shift in accordance with the roster issued by management. In addition, employees may be required to work overtime, including weekends, to meet operational requirements. **Pay Benefits** * Death\-in\-Service Benefit * On\-site parking * Vision/Eye Care **RATE OF PAY \-** DOE

title: Loan Administrator
description: Associate Loan Administrator – Dublin **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Job Type:** Full\-Time An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced **Associate Loan Administrator** to join a leading financial services organisation specialising in commercial real estate lending and loan servicing. This role offers the opportunity to work within a dynamic Loan Administration team, supporting a diverse portfolio of commercial real estate loans and working closely with Asset Management professionals to deliver exceptional service to clients. Key Responsibilities * Manage an allocated portfolio of commercial real estate loans. * Calculate and verify interest, principal repayments, and fees. * Prepare and issue borrower and lender notices accurately and on time. * Maintain loan records and data within loan administration systems. * Support the onboarding of newly closed loans. * Coordinate borrower bank account openings and related documentation. * Complete KYC and AML requirements associated with banking arrangements. * Respond to audit and loan confirmation requests. * Create, authorise, and process payments across multiple banking platforms. * Produce deal\-level reporting for securitised loans. * Maintain cash reconciliations, rent account reconciliations, and fee tracking records. Requirements * 3–5 years' experience within banking, loan administration, payments, or financial services. * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret financial information. * Advanced Excel and general IT proficiency. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work effectively both independently and within a team environment. * Proactive and detail\-oriented approach. * Knowledge of KYC, AML, and multi\-platform banking systems is advantageous. * University degree preferred. * Additional European language skills are desirable but not essential. What's on Offer * Opportunity to work with a highly regarded financial services team. * Exposure to complex commercial real estate transactions. * Collaborative and supportive working environment. * Career development opportunities within a growing international business. * Competitive salary and benefits package. If you have a strong background in loan administration, banking operations, or financial services and are looking for your next challenge in Dublin, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Loan Administrator Associate
description: Join a leading European credit and loan servicing business that partners with major financial institutions and real estate investors across complex lending portfolios. This is an opportunity to play a key role in the administration of commercial real estate loans, working at the heart of borrower, lender, and cash management activities while collaborating closely with asset management teams. What you'll be doing * Take ownership of a portfolio of commercial real estate loans from onboarding through ongoing administration. * Calculate and verify interest, principal, and fee payments with a high degree of accuracy. * Prepare and issue key borrower and lender communications and notices. * Manage loan data and maintain records within loan administration systems. * Support cash management activities, including payment processing, reconciliations, and reporting. * Coordinate account opening processes, KYC requirements, and audit\-related requests. * Contribute to the smooth delivery of servicing across complex lending structures and securitised transactions. What you'll bring * 3–5 years' experience in banking, loan administration, payments, or a related financial services environment. * Strong numerical and analytical skills with confidence working with financial data. * Advanced Excel capabilities and solid systems knowledge. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * A proactive, detail\-oriented approach and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. * Knowledge of KYC, AML, or multi\-bank account platforms is advantageous. * A university degree is preferred, and additional European language skills would be beneficial.

title: Manual Tester
description: **Manual Tester Initial 12 Month Contract Dublin / Hybrid (1 day per week required onsite) Rate €200 \- 280 per day** My client is looking for an experienced Manual Tester to support software testing activity across a large\-scale enterprise technology environment. This contract role would suit a QA professional with strong manual testing experience, excellent documentation skills, and the ability to work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders. The Job You will be responsible for reviewing requirements and design documentation, creating test scenarios and acceptance criteria, executing test cases, managing defects, and supporting user and business process testing. The role will involve working across system testing, regression testing, user acceptance testing, conformance testing, and disturbance testing. You will also help maintain traceability between requirements, acceptance criteria, test cases, and defects. **Key Responsibilities** * Review functional and technical design specifications for test readiness * Write clear, detailed, and testable acceptance criteria * Identify and define test data requirements * Create and execute test scenarios covering functional, non\-functional, regression, edge case, conformance, and disturbance testing * Raise, track, and manage defects using issue\-tracking tools * Support business users and stakeholders during UAT and business process testing * Maintain traceability between requirements, acceptance criteria, test cases, and defects * Document test processes, results, and quality records * Contribute to test estimation, planning, and delivery documentation * Review automated tests for coverage, quality, and effectiveness where required * Identify suitable areas for test automation * Mentor junior testers and support continuous improvement within the testing process **Have You:** * 5\+ years' professional experience in software testing or quality assurance * ISTQB certification * Strong manual testing experience across enterprise software environments * Strong understanding of testing principles, standards, and best practice * Experience creating and executing detailed test cases and test scenarios * Experience with system testing, UAT, regression testing, conformance testing, and defect management * Strong experience using issue\-tracking and test management tools * Good understanding of acceptance criteria, test data, requirements traceability, and quality documentation * Familiarity with API testing concepts and tools such as Postman or SOAP UI * Experience testing applications built on Java / Angular technology stacks * Working knowledge of SQL, XML, JSON, and Unix/Linux environments * Experience working within Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid delivery methodologies * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work effectively with technical teams, business users, and wider project stakeholders **Nice to Have** * Awareness of test automation approaches * Experience reviewing automated test coverage * Experience mentoring junior testers * Exposure to large\-scale public sector, financial services, or enterprise delivery environments **Desired Skills and Experience** Manual Tester Initial 12 Month Contract Dublin / Hybrid (1 day per week required onsite) Rate €200 \- 280 per day

title: Market Development Representative
description: **Company Overview** Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \#1 company in e\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM). What you'll do As a Market Development Representative (MDR), you will be responsible for generating qualified opportunities that progress through the sales funnel and ultimately generate new revenue to grow our presence in EMEA. This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Manager, MDR. **Responsibility** * Identify and generate new business opportunities and pipeline within a defined territory * Manage and plan your territory and outbound strategy strategically * Manage multi\-channel outbound prospecting to educate potential customers on what Docusign has to offer and gain insight into their business needs * Drive brand and product awareness in the French market through regular email and call campaigns * Schedule high\-impact appointments with those who are of good potential with Account Executives * Record prospect information in our CRM database * Present a balanced approach to cold calling, relationship development, sales process, and appointment setting Job Designation **Hybrid:** Employee divides their time between in\-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in\-office expectation) Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law. What you bring **Basic** * 1\+ years of related experience * Fluency in French and English * Experience with sales cycles and solution\-selling tactics * Experience with Salesforce CRM, Google Workspace, and other core business applications * Experience learning and adopting new technologies in a fast\-paced, AI\-first environment **Preferred** * Track record of success in outbound sales/business development role * Confident and courteous verbal and written communication skills * Excellent organizational skills * Familiarity with AI\-powered sales, research, and productivity tools, including Gong, LinkedIn, Glean, Gemini, Notebook LM * BS/BA degree or equivalent professional experience Life at Docusign **Working here** Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. title: Microbiology Analyst
description: **Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic, and labelling is accurate.** **Eurofins is dedicated to delivering testing services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, and to its corporate responsibility to protect the environment and ensure diversity, equity, and inclusion across the entire network of Eurofins companies.** **Eurofins PSS Insourcing Solutions® is a global, award\-winning insourcing solution. We place our skilled team directly at the client site, managing operations seamlessly and eliminating complexities. We ensure a strategic, long\-term service commitment that delivers value directly at the client’ site, utilising their facilities, equipment, and methodologies, while maintaining the same service, expertise, and cGMP compliance available at the Eurofins facility. If you're an employee seeking an exceptional career journey, Eurofins PSS Insourcing Solutions® is your gateway to a future where innovation, collaboration, and success converge. Join us in a world where excellence knows no boundaries!** **We’re proud to be an armed forces\-friendly place to work and welcome applications from reservists, armed forces veterans (including the wounded, injured and sick), as well as cadet instructors, and military spouses and partners.** **If you want to continue to help make our world a safer and healthier place, then apply for the role below and become a part of the extraordinary Eurofins network.** **Job Description** **Unfortunately, we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role.** **This is a full time, 12 Month Fixed Term Contract position. 39 hours per week, Working Monday \- Thursday 08:00 – 16:30 \& Friday 08:00 – 15:30\.** As a Microbiology Analyst you will work in a cross functional team to support the Microbiology Quality Control testing function, ensuring compliance with cGMP. **Job Responsibilities that you be covering:** * You will perform a broad range of microbiology testing including bioburden, endotoxin, microbial ID, Biological Indicator testing, BacT/ALERT, sterility checks, and growth‑promotion studies, while carrying out routine laboratory sampling, plate reading, data review, and trending. * You will conduct comprehensive environmental monitoring of cleanroom areas, including viable and non‑viable particulate monitoring and water and gas system monitoring, ensuring uniformity, reproducibility, and GMP‑compliant practices. * You will support and lead quality investigations related to deviations, prepare and revise protocols, reports, and SOPs, and review analytical data for GMP compliance with strong documentation accuracy. * You will collaborate closely with the client team, providing daily updates, maintaining strong working relationships, delivering excellent customer service, and ensuring all decisions align with GMP standards and customer expectations. * You will contribute to training, cross‑coverage, and continuous improvement by developing solutions to problems, identifying opportunities to enhance quality and service, updating documentation, and keeping training records current. * You will maintain a clean, safe, and audit‑ready laboratory environment, operate flexibly with shifting priorities, and consistently demonstrate strong organisation, communication, and problem‑solving skills while working both independently and as part of a team. **Qualifications** **What skills, qualifications and experiences you will hold as a Microbiology Analyst:** * You will hold a degree in Microbiology or a related discipline, along with at least one year of experience in a pharmaceutical regulatory environment. * You will bring hands‑on environmental monitoring experience, strong knowledge of cGMP requirements, and the ability to work independently while meeting established timelines. * You will have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to form strong working relationships and avoid misunderstandings in technical exchanges. * You will have a customer‑focused mindset, demonstrating ownership for speed, quality, and accuracy, along with a passion for quality and high service standards. * You will bring adaptability, resilience under pressure, and the ability to quickly learn new processes, switch priorities, and contribute effectively within a dynamic, growing team. * You will bring strong organisation, accuracy, and documentation skills, along with the capability to solve problems, work with limited supervision, and support continuous improvement. ****Additional Information**** At Eurofins we are growing, innovating and always learning. We celebrate the achievements of our employees through annual long service awards, recognise our colleagues special life events. **As a Eurofins employee you will benefit from:** * We offer exceptional opportunities for career growth, within a friendly and supportive team. As well as a competitive salary, pension and associated benefits you will enjoy an unrivalled working environment, including an active social calendar and subsidised canteen, all while working in a start of the art client facility\- a leading biotechnology company * Fantastic developmental opportunities and career progression pathways such as leadership, training, compliance and Subject Media Expert. * Full Benefits Package including:\- + Pension + Private Medical Insurance + Income protection and life assurance + Annual discretionary bonus + Birthday Holiday entitlement * Exciting Eurofins Incentives: Employee Activity Committee (EAC), Equality Driving Excellence (EDE), significant Reward and Recognition programme Opportunity to benefit from Eurofins Operational Development training platform, plus potential support for part\-time training (relevant to the position) * Full use of the canteen facilities and barista on the client site * Free parking onsite and onsite electric charging * Additional annual leave days with continued service * The opportunity to work on life saving products **What Happens Next** Our people are the backbone of what we do, so it's incredibly important we find the right individuals to join us. As a potential new recruit you'll be invited to meet the team in the form of an assessment centre or a staged interview process dependent on the role and it's requirements, this will give you the opportunity to see what working for Eurofins is really like and enable us to get you know your key skills and strengths. **Your data** As part of any recruitment process, the company collects and processes personal data relating to job applicants. The company is committed to being transparent about how it collects and uses that data and to meeting its data protection obligations. We embrace diversity! Eurofins network of companies believe in strength and innovation through diversity, being an Equal Opportunity Employer. We prohibit discrimination against employees or applications based on gender identity and/or expression, race, nationality, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and everything else that makes employees of Eurofins companies unique. **For more information about Eurofins PSS® please visit our** **website** **and watch a message below from our Global Head, Beth Di Paolo.**

title: Network Engineer
description: **Job requirements:\-** * **Required Qualifications** * Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Network Engineering, or a related field. * ·Experience: Proven hands\-on network engineering / administration experience, **15\+ years** * **Certifications (Desired):** * General: CompTIA Network\+ or Security\+ * Cloud: Cloud certifications beneficial (CCSK and/or an AWS certification) * **Technical Skills:\-** * · Deep understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, DNS, DHCP, MPLS, IPsec). · Hands\-on experience with enterprise\-grade equipment from vendors like Cisco, Aruba, and Palo Alto. * · Proficiency with network monitoring and diagnostic tools (e.g., Wireshark, SolarWinds). * · Knowledge of cloud networking and virtualization technologies. * · Familiarity with scripting and automation tools/languages (e.g., Python, Bash) is increasingly beneficial. * · Ability to follow precise company standards in design and configuration of new networks. * Soft Skills * · Strong analytical and complex problem\-solving abilities. * · Excellent written and verbal communication skills to explain technical issues to non\-technical stakeholders and also to clearly communicate with people with varying skills in English. * · Time management and project management skills to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines. * · Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * · Excellent attention to small details, both technical and logistical and differences between all global locations. * · Ability to work globally, sensitively and across many cultures and countries.

title: Network Security Engineer
description: **Who We Are** SiriusXM and its brands (Pandora, SXM Media, AdsWizz, Simplecast, and SiriusXM Connected Vehicle Services) are leading a new era of audio entertainment and services by delivering the most compelling subscription and ad\-supported audio entertainment experience for listeners \-\- in the car, at home, and anywhere on the go with connected devices. Our vision is to shape the future of audio, where everyone can be effortlessly connected to the voices, stories and music they love wherever they are. We are proud to have launched a new state\-of\-the\-art technology facility in Dublin, Ireland. The Dublin\-based team plays a critical role in our continued digital transformation, and functions as a center of excellence for SiriusXM’s global Product and Technology organization. SiriusXM’s new Dublin facility comprises a workforce focused on software development, automotive technology and engineering, adtech, data science, and analytics. Talent based in Dublin will be charged with developing and implementing best\-in\-class standards for high\-quality, scalable software deliveries for SiriusXM’s streaming and in\-vehicle audio entertainment platforms. **How You'll Make An Impact** The Enterprise Network \& Security team designs, secures, and operates the enterprise\-facing infrastructure that provides critical interconnectivity for employees and partners. We are seeking a **Network Security Engineer** to strengthen our global security posture by focusing on the design, implementation, and management of next\-generation firewalls, load balancers, and other network security platforms. The successful candidate will lead efforts to enforce security policies, improve resiliency, and ensure compliance across broadcast, data center, and enterprise network environments, while also mentoring peers and driving innovation in security best practices. **What You'll Do** * Design, implement, and manage enterprise firewalls, load balancers, and related network security platforms. * Deploy, monitor, and optimize network security controls across enterprise, broadcast, and data center networks. * Triage, diagnose, and resolve firewall, load balancing, and VPN\-related incidents. * Ensure compliance with InfoSec requirements and industry\-standard security frameworks. * Drive automation efforts to standardize firewall and security policy management (“security as code”). * Collaborate with InfoSec, broadcast engineering, and enterprise infrastructure teams to enforce security policies and respond to new requirements. * Create and maintain documentation for security standards, policies, and operational procedures. * Provide subject matter expertise on network security to cross\-functional engineering teams. * Participate in on\-call rotation for operational network and security support. **What You'll Need** * Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field. * 3\+ years of experience working in enterprise network security environments. * Excellent interpersonal, communication, and presentation skills. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in high\-pressure environments. * Strong organizational skills with the ability to handle multiple priorities. * Commitment to delivering high\-quality internal customer service. * Proficiency with documentation and productivity tools such as Google Workspace, LucidChart/Visio, and MS Office. * Hands\-on expertise with Palo Alto firewalls, VPN technologies (GlobalProtect, AnyConnect, IPsec), and security policy enforcement. * Strong experience with F5 load balancers (LTM/GTM/ASM/AFM) and traffic management policies. * Knowledge of Cisco ASA firewalls and other major vendor platforms (Juniper, Arista, etc.). * Familiarity with zero\-trust access control solutions (ClearPass, Cisco ISE). * Solid understanding of network protocols (TCP/UDP, IPv4/IPv6, BGP, OSPF, VXLAN, EVPN, IP Multicast). * Experience with automation frameworks (Python, Ansible, Terraform, Go) for network security operations. * Skilled in network monitoring and troubleshooting tools (Wireshark, NetFlow, Syslog; OpenNMS, SolarWinds, or Datadog a plus). * Ability to take ownership of critical issues through resolution and support on\-call rotations. ***Our goal at SiriusXM is to provide and maintain a work environment that fosters mutual respect, professionalism and cooperation. SiriusXM is an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate on the basis of actual or perceived race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, alienage or citizenship status, age, disability or handicap, sex, gender identity, marital status, familial status, veteran status, sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws.*** The requirements and duties described above may be modified or waived by the Company in its sole discretion without notice. R\-2025\-11\-14

This includes low voltage drives, PLC \& HMI, and IEC low voltage motors offering to different types of OEMs and machine builders across different segments and applications within the Irish and Northern Irish market. Delivers both qualitative and quantitative sales targets to ensure sustainable order growth, revenues, profitability, market share, and customer satisfaction. impact. * Work model: Office based with travel and flexibility. * Review the local market, identify and execute predefined market strategies. * Drive volume, mix, and profitability targets for selected customers across the division. * Build and maintain strategic customer relationships to understand their needs and position ABB's drive product solutions. * Leverage cross\-division synergies to enhance customer value and ensure a seamless sales experience. * Develop short\-to\-medium term sales plans using ABB tools. * Conduct regular pipeline reviews and propose recovery actions for any shortfalls. * Prepare competitive offers in coordination with the bid and proposal team, ensuring technical and financial accuracy. * Validate pricing, trading conditions, and margins on all proposals. * Communicate delivery timelines, payment terms, and contractual conditions aligned with ABB strategy. * Develop and communicate value propositions through marketing activities. * Manage sales administration procedures, commercial decisions, and support project management as needed. **Qualifications For The Role** * Degree in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field * Marketing / Business qualification or some time working in a similar business environment with a track record of building customer relationships considered a bonus * Previous experience in engaging business to position ABB as a valued solutions provider vrs competitors * Results\-oriented mindset combined with the engineering and business acumen * Strategic in approach and confidence to take initiative, and turn ideas into action * Strong communication and collaboration skills with the ability to build trust across stakeholders * Genuine drive to learn, grow, and continuously improve, both personally and as a sales individual **Benefits** ABB offers a full range of benefits to help you thrive at work and beyond. * Competitive Salary * Private Medical Allowance for Yourself and Family * Competitive Contributory Pension Scheme * Employee Share Acquisition Plan * Internal Mobility Opportunities, Mentorships \& Access to Projects Globally * Employee Assistance Programme * Generous Employee Referral Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave Plus Bank Holidays * Company Car * Flexible Working * Generous Bonus Scheme **More About Us** ABB's Drive Products and IEC Low Voltage Motors division delivers motors, integrated motors, drives, and automation solutions for OEMs and machine builders globally. Our comprehensive portfolio combines ultra premium\-efficiency IEC Low Voltage Motors, variable frequency drives, AC500 PLCs, and HMIs to create optimised automation packages that reduce costs, reduce downtime, improve process throughput, and lower operational carbon footprint. With electric motors consuming 45% of global electricity and high\-efficiency systems cutting consumption by 10%, our integrated solutions help OEMs across blue\-chip manufacturing, water, food \& beverage, mining, and data centers build smarter, more efficient machines and operations. Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact?
