title: Office Administrator
description: Sherry FitzGerald Geraldine O’Reilly in Virginia, Co Cavan are recruiting for a full\-time office administrator to provide a professional, efficient administrative support service to their team. The role is based in our Virginia office. Role \& Responsibilities * Provide an excellent level of customer service. * To provide administrative assistance and support to the Managing Director and the overall team. * Responsible for dealing with all incoming phone, email, mySherryFitz queries relating to the office such as arranging viewing appointments, market appraisals etc in a friendly and helpful manner. * Ensure timely responses and that all details are recorded accurately on the relevant RPS (CRM system) file. * Prepare Terms of Engagement and ensure all compliance documentation is on file in advance of property being made available. * Prepare Digital MA kits. * Coordinate and manage marketing for sales properties – boards, photographs, brochures, newspaper adverts, social media. * Maintain organised, easy to access files on computer ensuring all files are complaint with GDPR, AML and PSRA regulation. * Order photos \& download to the relevant property file. * Update all window displays \& ensure display screens in branch are kept updated. **Key Skills \& Attributes** * Friendly and approachable demeanour with strong interpersonal skills. * Customer\-focused with a genuine desire to help and support others. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Strong organisational and time management skills. * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) and general IT systems. * Discretion and confidentiality in handling sensitive information. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Previous experience in an administrative or front\-of\-house role preferred. * Experience working in a customer service environment is a plus.

title: Online Fulfilment Assistant
description: We are looking for individuals to join our exciting Boots.ie order picking team in\-store. You will work in a Boots store and report into the Assistant or Store Manager and your role will be to pick and pack customer orders, work in our back shop, run our stock room and support with deliveries and stock management. **About The Role** To support our Boots.ie operation we have implemented several programs nationally in\-store to ensure we meet the demand of our customer’s needs. As part of this role, you will: * Pick and pack online customer orders to a high standard using a handheld device * Obtain the relevant information required for picking, packing \& dispatching of orders. * Locate all the required goods in the picking areas across the store. * Use the Pod equipment safely and effectively * Comply with the organisation’s policies and procedures and all relevant legal, safety and operating requirements relating to picking, packing and dispatching of online orders * Support the wider store operational activities **What You’ll Need To Have** Full training is provided for this role and these are the essential skills or experience needed to succeed in this role: * Understanding of an online pick/pack operation * Enjoy following process and working at pace * A problem solving mindset * Enjoy working collaboratively as part of a team. * Able to communicate and listen effectively. * Digitally savvy and comfortable embracing new technology, including handheld mobile devices to process online orders * A passion for providing great customer care \- while most of your work will happen behind the scenes, you may occasionally support out on the shop floor working with customers. * A flexible approach to working hours and patterns **It would be great if you also have** These are desirable skills or experience and are not essential, so we would welcome applications from candidates that don’t match this additional criteria. * Experience in a retail or warehouse operations environment * Experience with inventory systems management * Familiar with handheld Pods and IT systems **Our Benefits** * Pension membership (PRSA) * Partial paid maternity leave * Discretionary annual bonus * Generous employee discounts * Flexible benefits scheme We have a great range of benefits in addition to the above that offer flexibility to suit you \- find out more at **boots.jobs/ireland** . Exclusions may apply, eligible roles only. *Please note, any salary estimates given on third\-party sites are not provided or endorsed by Boots and may not be accurate.* **Why Boots** At Boots, we foster a working environment where consideration and inclusivity help everyone to be themselves and reach their full potential. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, passionate about embracing the diversity of our colleagues and providing a positive and inclusive working environment for all. As the heart of everything we do at Boots, with you, we change for the better. **What's Next** If your application is successful, you'll be invited to an in\-store interview in the next 14 days. Where a role is advertised as full\-time, we are open to discussing part\-time and job share options during the application process. If you require additional support as part of the application and interview process, we are happy to provide **reasonable adjustments** to help you to be at your best.

title: Operations & Outsourcing Co-Ordinator (12 month Fixed Term Contract)
description: Fineco Asset Management DAC (‘Fineco AM’) is a Dublin based Asset Management Company, and asubsidiary of FinecoBank S.p.A. (“FinecoBank”). FinecoBank (“Fineco”) is a leading European bank with a 20\-year history and a fully digital DNA with a branchless approach since the start. Fineco is one of the banks with the widest products range available. Fineco have developed a truly one\-stop\-solution which includes trading, investment and payment services. Fineco have developed a 100% proprietary platform for trading and investment which covers all customer needs from trading listed and OTC product to investment product such as mutual funds and ETF. Fineco AM, which started its operations in May 2018, is authorized by the Central Bank of Ireland as a UCITS \& AIFM Management Company with a MIFID top up, and it currently has approximately €39bn in assets under management.Fineco AM is a competence centre\- specialised in the management of fund\-of\-funds, single strategy funds, as well as Structured Solutions Funds. In addition to portfolio management activities, all key supporting and controlling functions, such as Compliance, Risk, Finance and Legal, are also performed locally. The company started with 5 employees and has experienced strong and consistent growth, with a current headcount of approximately 90 FTE’s. Currently Fineco AM manages 3 UCITS Umbrella Funds – 1 in Luxembourg and 2 in Ireland **Main Responsibilities:** We are looking to recruit enthusiastic professionals, reporting to the Head of Operations to support our UCITS Management Company business. * Be the first point of contact with the investment managers, in relation to outstanding issues as they arise, including settlement, invoicing of the funds and cash flows; * Be the first point of contact with custodian and the fund administrator, in relation to outstanding matters regarding settlement and dividend distribution and any other corporate actions; * Liaise with custodian(s) to open new accounts on behalf of sub\-funds, sub\-Advisors, etc. * Liaise with tax advisors to ensure proper and timely WHT filing/CGT rate applied and rebates reclaimed. * Support overall delegate/outsourced service oversight programme in accordance with company’s internal policies and procedures * Manage the end\-to\-end operational set\-up requirements for static data, notifications, filings, etc. for new fund launches * Collect, review and validate fund expense fees including administration, custody, other fund service providers whilst ensuring they are in line with our funds’ General Expense Budgets; manage communications with investment managers and service providers to ensure timely and accurate payment * Contribute to the production and maintenance of a General Expenses budget for the funds and review, on a periodic basis accruals in the funds NAVs against invoiced amounts. * Assist in the periodic review of NAVs, EMIR Reporting. Ongoing Charges etc. as produced by the Firm’s delegates. Report, track to resolution any exceptions observed. **Key Requirements:** * Third level qualification in Accountancy, Finance or a Business discipline. * 2\-3 years' experience in Operations/Middle Office/Custody. * Good knowledge of financial products and financial markets. * Very good knowledge of Excel. * Working knowledge of AI tools an advantage. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Strong attention to detail. * Well organized with strong time management skills. * Good team player. * Be enthusiastic and eager to learn, self\-starter. **What we offer:** * **Growth Opportunities:** Unlock your potential with career growth and professional development paths. * **Innovative Team:** Be part of a dynamic and innovative team environment. * **Collaborative Culture:** Thrive in a collaborative company culture that values your contributions. * **Generous Time Off:** Enjoy 25 days of holidays to relax and recharge. * **Hybrid Working:** Benefit from a flexible hybrid working policy, 3 days in the office, 2 days flexibility to be remote. * **Health and Dental:** Comprehensive Healthcare and Dental coverage for your well\-being.

title: Operations Summer Intern
description: ***About the Role*** As a Student Operations Intern, you will support the full lifecycle of a Greystar student community. This role blends on\-site resident hospitality with operational and commercial management, focusing on the unique needs of the student demographic and the critical Summer period. Please note that this is a 10\-week internship during June and August. ***Key Responsibilities*** * **Full Lifecycle Support:** Own the administrative journey from initial enquiry and room viewings to contract signing, guarantor verification, and the high\-intensity "Summer Turn" periods. * **Front\-of\-House Query Management:** Act as the first point of contact for all resident enquiries, resolving issues ranging from parcel collection and maintenance requests to account queries with speed and empathy. * **Leasing \& Compliance Admin:** Maintain accurate resident records within property management systems ensuring all tenancy documentation and right\-to\-rent checks meet compliance standards. * **Summer Short\-Stay Operations:** Support the transition to short\-term tenancies during the summer months, managing high\-frequency check\-ins for interns and summer school groups to maximize asset occupancy. * **Head Office Operational Support:** Bridge the gap between site and Head Office by assisting the Operations team with portfolio\-wide data entry, occupancy reporting, and process improvement projects. * **Operational \& Financial Admin:** Support lease processing, guarantor verification, and rent collection. Gain exposure to reporting, financial and compliance standards (Maintenance/Facilities/Health \& Safety) to ensure asset protection. ***About You*** * **The Student Expert:** Currently enrolled in a degree (Real Estate, Hospitality, or Business) with an interest in the Living sector. * **Agile Communicator:** Comfortable pivoting from supporting a student to presenting data to an Operations Manager * **Tech\-Fluent:** Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and ready to learn specialised platforms like Yardi or Entrata ***About Greystar*** *Greystar is a leading, fully integrated real estate company offering expertise in investment management, development, and management of rental housing properties globally. The company's business model is unique in its ability to own, operate, and develop multifamily, student, and senior housing across the globe. Building a global platform with a local presence, Greystar continues to expand its geographic reach around the world, bringing a globally interconnected professional rental housing platform and industry funded with institutional capital to countries where the sector does not yet exist. Greystar’s pan – European platform was established in 2013 and has scaled rapidly, now with investment, operational and development presence in the UK, Ireland, France, the Netherlands, Germany, Austria, and Spain.* *One of Greystar’s core values is Equality, and as such, we continue to pride ourselves on being an equal opportunities employer, accounting for all protected and identifiable characteristics. We truly value diversity of thought, background and of experience. Please reach out to the Talent Acquisition team to explore opportunities at Greystar in more detail or visit our careers site at https://jobs.greystar.com/.*

title: Order Management Specialist
description: **The opportunity** Reporting to our Market Manager for Ireland, the **Order Management Specialist** will help to shape our customer experience by supporting the smooth and accurate processing of orders, from confirmation through to delivery. This position is based at our Dublin Office in Liffey Valley. This entry‑level position works closely with sales, logistics, supply chain teams, vendors, and customers contributing to the effective management of orders, timely resolution of queries, and the delivery of consistent service standards. You’ll be encouraged to take ownership of your work, learn through experience, and contribute ideas that support the wider team and business. We will provide hands\-on training and development for this role. **How you’ll make An Impact** * Ensure timely release of orders and a fulfilment of pre\-established standards and policies to achieve customer satisfaction. * Monitor and resolve order issues and process errors. * Act as the point of contact for clients with queries, quotes, order processing. * Work with the Supply Chain team on order management and shipping support. * Prepare dispatch and shipping documentation for customers, qualify payments. * Enter orders into SAP and maintain order databases. * Create purchase orders for suppliers and internal work orders. * Work with wider Sales and Project **Your background** * Previous experience in a Customer Service/Administrative/Order Processing environment desired, not essential * A keen eye for attention to detail and accuracy * The ability to communicate effectively, over the phone, via email and face to face * Good PC skills, including Microsoft Office suite, specifically Outlook, Word and Excel desired Publication date : 2026\-06\-15

title: Part Time Seasonal Invigilator
description: **Job Opportunity at IMMA** **Open Competition for the role of** **X8 Part Time Seasonal Invigilator** **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **About IMMA** The Irish Museum of Modern Art, based in what was the Royal Hospital Kilmainham, an extraordinary 17th century building, set upon a hilltop of 48 acres overlooking the river Liffey, is a witness to at least a millennium of change. All of which feeds into our contemporary thinking and commitment to celebrate this remarkable site. At IMMA we have begun to create a space unique with contemporary culture. This is a space where local and global connect, a space where contemporary art and contemporary life meet, challenge and inspire one another. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Role Overview** To work as part of the Visitor Experience Team to deliver a high standard of customer service and create a welcoming, engaging, and safe environment for all visitors and employees, while ensuring the museum, its property, assets, and surrounding areas are maintained securely through vigilance and due diligence. To maintain a professional and approachable manner at all times, delivering excellent customer service by engaging with visitors and providing clear, relevant information on the museum’s galleries, exhibitions, artists, and public programmes. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Key Responsibilities** *Key responsibilities to be undertaken based on priorities agreed with HOD and subject to the needs of the Department/ Programme.* * Proactively engage with museum guests to deliver an excellent standard of customer service, creating a welcoming, informative, and positive visitor experience for all. * Will safeguard the museum’s property from Theft and Damage at all times and ensure that the museums visitor guidelines are carried out at all times through effective communication and invigilation. * Ensuring that all Galleries and exhibitions are open and ready to admit visitors on time by good time keeping and teamwork. * Ensure all visitors to IMMA are treated with respect and as far as practicable have enjoyed their experience though staff interaction with them. * Will be capable of invigilation and associated duties for exhibitions and events held at the Museum or other locations that may be utilised temporarily for the Irish Museum of Modern Arts programmes. * Will undergo all relevant training, relating to staff and visitor emergency evacuation procedures * Dealing with all visitors in responding positively in providing information and promoting the museum’s current and forthcoming programs. * Promotion of the museum’s patrons and members programs, current publications and forth coming talks and lectures when working in the galleries or Front of House **Communication** * To be capable of communication and interaction with visitors on all levels * To be capable of communication professionally with all colleagues **Programme Evaluation** * Liaising with the departments Supervisors and where practicable with Curators to provide accurate mid exhibition feedback and Post Exhibition feedback of the visitor experience **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Key Performance Indicators:** * Will be capable of assisting in the physical evacuation and subsequent security of the museum in the event of fire evacuation or any other emergency that may affect the operation of the museum. * Will be able to deal with the public and respond positively in providing relevant information on the museum’s current and forthcoming programmes. * Will be a team player and capable of working within the team to achieve the goals that have been set out within the museums strategy statement. * Maintain a proactive contribution to ensuring that IMMA provides a welcoming, informative and accommodating experience for all visitors * A commitment to engaging with the public on the gallery floor with the necessary information and skills to deliver information to the best of their ability. * Proactive invigilation and communicative skills utilised in protecting exhibitions * Will undertake statutory and relevant training as directed by the museum. Including mandatory Child Protection Training * Punctuality and reliability in working as a team player within the Visitor Engagement Team to deliver the department and IMMA’s goals **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **The successful candidate will have:** * Excellent communication skills and the ability to work as part of a team. * Experience in a customer focused role. Relative experience in the museum or tourist/hospitality sector an advantage. * Experience with computers to ECDL standard or similar. * Confident in asserting themselves when dealing with emergencies such as fire evacuation. * Empathetic and capable of understanding a range of visitor’s needs. Particularly in more challenging situations. * Experience working as part of a friendly and interpersonal team. * The ability to work under pressure and multi\-task. * An interest in the visual arts is an advantage. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Why Work with IMMA?** **At IMMA, we are proud to offer:** * Opportunity to get involved in IMMA’s 2024\-2028 Strategy, development of key themes in response to current global cultural concerns which will shape our programming over the next five years. * The opportunity to work in a beautiful serene historic 17th Century Building surrounded by 48 acres of Meadows and Gardens – an oasis in Dublin City. * Free Parking / Secure Bike Sheds. * Career development opportunities through mentoring, coaching, and training. * Government Pension Scheme * Employee Wellbeing Committee. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Generous Public Sector leave benefits. * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture. * Strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. * Great team environment working to our Core Tenets: Contemporary, Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration and Globally Connected. * Valuable experience at a leading cultural institution. * Opportunities to attend special museum events and exhibitions. * Networking opportunities with professionals in the museum and cultural sectors. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Salary** The salary for this role is in line with public sector pay scales, starting at **€36,227**, Point 1 of the Grade **Invigilator** (PPC) scale, as of 1st June 2026\. The Full Time Salary Scale: €36,227; €37,297; €38,369; €39,300; €39,875; €40,630; €41,209 Candidates should note the Irish Museum of Modern Art complies with Department of Public Expenditure and Reform and guidelines on Public Sector pay and conditions of employment and pension provisions. Entry level will be at Point 1 of this Grade. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Contract Details** * **Contract Type:** Fixed Term * **Contract Duration:** 6 months * **Hours:** 20 hours a month, breaks included * **Location:** IMMA, Military Road, The Royal Hospital Kilmainham, Kilmainham, Dublin 8 **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **How to Apply** To apply for this exciting opportunity, please submit the following: * A tailored cover letter explaining your suitability for the role. * A current curriculum vitae. Applications should be submitted via https://imma.bamboohr.com/careers/75 **Closing date for receipt of applications**: 29/06/2026 **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Please note:** * IMMA operates an open competition process, and applications are accepted exclusively via BambooHR. * Applications sent by email or through other channels will not be considered. For questions related to the role or application process, you are welcome to contact us. * IMMA’s HR office is closed on Saturdays, Sundays, and Irish Bank Holidays. Any queries submitted during these times will be responded to on the next working day. * We aim to respond to queries as promptly as possible. However, during periods of high activity, response times may be longer than usual. We appreciate your patience and understanding, and we thank you for your interest in working with IMMA. For further assistance or reasonable accommodations during the application process, please email tetiana.shchukina@imma.ie. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Join Our Team** IMMA is an equal\-opportunity employer, deeply committed to promoting equality, diversity, and inclusion. We actively encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds and experiences. We value the richness that a diverse team brings to our work, enhancing both staff and visitor experiences. Full Role Profile available at the career website: https://imma.ie/about/jobs\-opportunities/current\-vacancies/

title: Personal Lines Executive
description: Job description The ideal candidate will possess strong product knowledge, sales, interpersonal and organizational skills. They should be comfortable with multitasking and be able to work productively in a fast paced role: **Main Responsibilities:** • Looking after existing business, new business and midterm adjustments • Reviewing policy renewals, policy wording, endorsements, and customer service on accounts. • Must be willing to take on cases efficiently and proactively from beginning to completion. • Negotiate renewals, re\-marketing and finalising insurance placements and provide coverage comparisons when making recommendations to clients. • Ensure that compliance procedures are always adhered to. • Build effective and lasting relationships with key personnel in all insurance providers. • Provide a friendly and professional service to our clients with a focus on communicating decisions in a prompt and professional manner. **Skills \& Experience:** • Minimum CIP qualification or working towards this • Ability to work independently and as a team member. • Strong attention to detail and high level of organisation and accuracy with the ability to provide comprehensive updates to company directors on all cases. • Excellent IT Skills (Excel, Word and Report preparation) • Ability to work under pressure, prioritize and meet tight deadlines. • Must be able to engage with clients, be a good listener, listen to their requirements and provide effective insurance solutions with their best interest in mind. • Preferably have 2/3 years’ experience in a similar role • Be a good team player who is highly motivated and enthusiastic • Have Relay Software experience We offer an attractive remuneration package, a range of benefits and reward highly motivated and dedicated staff with great career opportunities. If you feel this is the right opportunity for you, email your CV and cover letter to colin@spmfinancialservices.ie and we will be in touch.

title: Pre-Sales and Installation Engineer
description: **Company Description:** Founded in 1996, Pacific Computers Ltd have evolved into a leading 21st century digital technology partner, a single port of call for IT hardware, software and AV requirements. Pacific are highly experienced in designing, installing and supporting a wide range of audio\-visual technology based systems, bringing them all together into a single seamless and yet flexible solution. Pacific’s company ethos is centred on delivering excellence and this is evidenced by the quality of our people. We only use in\-house staff, from Pre\-Sales design and consultancy, through to support and tender responses, which allows us to maintain a consistent level of service to all our end users. We continually invest significant time and effort in to our staff, this ensures that their knowledge and experience is at the leading edge of both current and developing technologies, allowing us to install and support the highest quality IT and Audio\-Visual systems to a broad spectrum of companies and organisations internationally. As a company Pacific are ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015 and Safe Contractor certified, giving customers the peace of mind that they are dealing with a high quality and reliable company. We are also an HP Gold Partner, Dell Gold Partner, Crestron Elite Partner, Extron Accredited Partner and Canon ProAV Advanced Partner, which demonstrates our robust partner relationships. **Role Description:** The position available is for a quality member of our Audio\-Visual Pre\-Sales and installations engineering team. As a member of the team, you will be responsible for designing solutions to meet the customers’ requirements and at times, carrying out the installation. Part of this process will involve creating technical drawings and writing up the functionality of the system in order to make up part of the proposal documentation. Another aspect of your responsibilities will involve being on hand for the sales team to carry out site surveys to ensure all aspects of the customers’ requirements are met and the solution proposed technically fits the environment, and specifying the correct amount of labour required to carry out the installation successfully. The role will also involve, on the occasion, being part of an installation team as an engineer to carry out the installation. An element of project management is also required in this role, as you will often be expected to attend project meetings and ensure installations are carried out to a high standard and handed over to the client in a professional manner. **Main Tasks and Responsibilities:** · Design Audio\-Visual solutions to meet customer requirements. · Producing an equipment list of everything required for the solution specified. · Produce schematics and technical documentation. · Installation engineering to complete AV installations. · Attending meetings with business development managers, acting as a technical expert. · Respond to new customer enquires both by phone and email. · Help enhance customer relationships by attending meetings with members of our sales team. **Required Experience:** · Minimum of 2 years in a similar Pre\-Sales role. · Minimum of 2 years working in a customer facing role. · Minimum of 2 years’ experience as an Audio Visual install engineer. · Good knowledge of AV technologies, including VC and UC. · An ability to communicate technical information to non\-technical staff across all levels in a way that is easy to understand. · Able to produce accurate schematics/technical drawings from customer requirements. · The ability to work under pressure and to timescales, often working on multiple projects simultaneously. **Advatages:** · CTS, CTS\-D or CTS\-I qualification. · ECS certification. · Previous experience in a similar role. · Project management experience. **Key Competencies:** · Excellent communication skills. · An in\-depth understanding of AV technologies. · Engineering background. · Lateral thinking and problem\-solving skills. · Works well both on their own and as part of a team. · Flexible and adaptable. · A full and clean driving license. . Awareness of emerging technologies

title: Pricing Analyst
description: **Base Location:** You'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in either our Dublin or Belfast office **Salary:** €49,400 \- €74,200 (RoI) or £35,200 \- £52,800 (NI) \+ performance\-related bonus \+ a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Working Pattern:** Permanent \| Full Time \| Flexible First options available **The role** Our Commercial function drives forward the strategic vision for SSE Airtricity by providing optimised outcomes that balance risk and reward. A typical day might involve reviewing the wholesale energy markets to understand how current market prices may impact the price that we need to charge our customers and calculating the profitability of our current product offerings. This role will give the successful candidate an excellent understanding of the electricity and gas retail markets across Ireland along with an appreciation of the end\-to\-end business processes required to deliver complex energy products to B2B customers. **You will** * Support with B2B pricing strategy recommendations for new and existing products to enable us to deliver our strategic plan, whilst managing and mitigating risks. * Provide pricing support to SME Sales to ensure that offerings are reflective of market conditions and in line with commercial guidelines. * Manage trade requests in relation to Major Business customers on flexible contracts and ensure that a clear record of trades is maintained and that accurate prices are generated. * Support with respect to the invoicing of Major Business customers who have complex requirements which involve manual billing. **You have** * A commercial mindset, with strong attention to detail and the ability to break down complex problems to fully understand and deliver improved and effective solutions. * Proven experience building Power BI dashboards. * Excellent Excel skills. * Demonstrable experience working with complex data sets and modelling. * Strong commercial acumen and a solid understanding of financial principles. **About SSE** SSE’s purpose is to provide energy needed today while building a better world of energy for tomorrow. We do this by developing, building, operating and investing in electricity infrastructure and businesses needed in the energy transition. Our Transforming for Growth investment plan sees us investing £33bn in critical electricity infrastructure across the five years to 2030\. With over 725,000 electricity and gas customers across the island of Ireland, SSE Airtricity helps homes and businesses on the journey to net zero. We're more than just an energy provider, delivering energy saving services to homes and businesses, helping them become more efficient and cost\-effective. **Flexible benefits to fit your life** Enjoy discounts on private healthcare and gym memberships. Wellbeing benefits like a free online GP and 24/7 counselling service. Interest\-free loans on tech and transport season tickets, or a new bike with our Cycle to Work scheme. As well as generous family entitlements such as maternity and adoption pay, and paternity leave. **Work with an equal opportunity employer** SSE will make any reasonable adjustments you need to ensure that your application and experience with us is positive. Please contact Andy on andrew.elder3@sse.com / 01738 340826 to discuss how we can support you. We're dedicated to fostering an open and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We create equal opportunities for everyone to succeed and especially welcome applications from those who may not be well represented in our workforce or industry. **Ready to apply?** Start your online application using the Apply Now box on this page. We only accept applications made online. We'll be in touch after the closing date to let you know if we'll be taking your application further. \#LI\-AIR \#LI\-AE1 \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Private Credit Analyst/Associate
description: We are partnering with a well established Pan European private debt platform to hire an Investment **Analyst** for its Dublin based investment team. This is a strong opportunity to join an active credit investor focused on mid\-cap corporate transactions across Ireland, the DACH region, and broader continental Europe. The strategy spans sponsored and non\-sponsored deals across the capital structure, including Senior Secured, Unitranche, Mezzanine, and Minority Equity. The successful candidate will support deal professionals across the full investment lifecycle from origination and due diligence through execution and ongoing portfolio monitoring. **The Role:** Investment Process * Reviewing information memoranda and third\-party due diligence reports * Preparing investment committee materials * Building and maintaining detailed financial models (including scenario and sensitivity analysis) * Conducting comparable transaction and precedent financing analysis * Performing market and desktop research Execution * Supporting term sheet discussions and reviewing legal documentation * Assisting with transaction closing and funding * Ongoing portfolio monitoring and performance tracking Additional Responsibilities * Preparing investor marketing materials * Maintaining pipeline trackers and internal systems * Assisting with NDAs, release letters, and related documentation **Key Requirements:** * 2–5 years’ experience in leveraged finance, corporate credit, corporate finance, or investment banking * Proactive, with a willingness to take on responsibilities beyond core day\-to\-day tasks * High level of attention to detail * Advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint * Excellent academic record and strong interpersonal skills * Additional languages such as German, Italian, or Spanish are advantageous

description: **About Agoda** At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. title: Public Procurement Lawyer
description: A\&L Goodbody (ALG) is currently seeking to hire a qualified solicitor **(with 2\-5 PQE)** to join our market\-leading EU, Competition \& Procurement team, with a particular focus on public procurement law. This is an opportunity to develop within a market\-leading public procurement practice, advising on complex and strategically significant matters for public and private sector clients. **The Team** Our team brings many years of private and public experience advising on EU, competition and public procurement law. The team are leaders in Ireland in their fields of expertise providing clients with strategic advice and advocacy across these areas, and for its leading position in the Irish market. We advise on all aspects of EU, competition \& procurement law, working with an impressive client base that includes domestic and global corporations, as well as state and semi\-state bodies. Our market\-leading procurement practice supports contracting authorities, utilities and bidders across a wide range of sectors, advising on complex, high\-value and strategically important projects. Our practice is characterised by close collaboration, not just within the team, but across the firm. We work seamlessly with our Projects \& Infrastructure, Construction \& Engineering and Corporate groups, as well as our Disputes and Investigations group, to deliver practical, commercially focused advice and support throughout the procurement lifecycle. Collaboration is central to how we work at ALG. Our open\-door culture means you will have direct access to partners and senior colleagues from day one, strong training and supervision, and opportunities to take meaningful responsibility while contributing to knowledge\-sharing initiatives and cross\-departmental projects. This integrated approach provides excellent exposure to major public sector and regulated procurement mandates. Working alongside our multidisciplinary Client Technology team, we also leverage innovative legal technology and tools to support efficient procurement processes and contract management solutions for our clients. **The Role** In this role, you will develop your skills and experience across a broad range of public procurement matters, including: * Advising contracting authorities and utilities on the design and implementation of procurement processes * Supporting bidders through all stages of tender processes, including strategy, clarification questions and submissions * Drafting and advising on tender documentation, framework agreements and public contracts * Advising on compliance with EU and Irish procurement rules, including remedies and standstill obligations * Managing procurement\-related challenges and litigation before the Irish courts * Advising on modifications to public contracts and ongoing contract management issues * Supporting on strategic projects involving infrastructure, energy, technology and public services * Advising on the interaction between procurement law and broader regulatory frameworks including State aid and the Foreign Subsidies Regulation * Preparing and delivering client training, public speaking engagements, presentations and knowledge initiatives on procurement law developments Ireland's continued investment in infrastructure and public services, alongside evolving EU procurement regulations, makes procurement law a dynamic and growing area. You will gain first\-hand experience advising on high\-profile procurement processes and disputes, working closely with both public sector clients and private sector participants. **Career Development and Growth** We are committed to investing in our people, and our expanding practice offers genuine opportunities for progression. As a member of the team, you will benefit from structured training, supervision, early responsibility on significant matters, direct client exposure and opportunities to build your profile through presentations and public speaking. You will have the opportunity to grow within an established market\-leading practice that is going from strength to strength. **Requirements** * Qualified lawyers with 2\-5 years' post\-qualification experience * Strong experience or a demonstrated interest in public procurement law * Experience in a large or medium corporate law firm in Ireland or the United Kingdom * An excellent academic record * Strong interpersonal and communication skills * A collaborative mindset and strong team\-working abilities * Excellent organisational and prioritisation skills **Benefits** * A competitive remuneration package * Access to the firm's marquee training and development programmes, delivered through the ALG School of Business and Law * Discretionary performance bonus * Pension and health insurance contribution, wellbeing contribution, life assurance, subsidised gym, restaurant and coffee shop * Hybrid working policy **The Firm** ALG is a leading Irish law firm with five international offices in Dublin, Belfast, London, New York and San Francisco. There are currently 127 partners and over 1,000 employees. The strong international and domestic business community in Ireland forms a valuable part of the ALG client base. ALG is incredibly proud to be recognised as one of the leading Irish law firms in the Chambers and Partners Europe 2026 Guide. ALG is also ranked as a 'top tier' law firm in The Legal 500 EMEA Guide 2026 and the IFLR1000 Guide 2026\. The firm is consistently ranked number one in the Irish market and on a European level. ALG has a reputation for leading the way in continuously developing its service to clients and internally to its business. This is delivered using new processes, ways of working, innovative use of technology and marketing\-leading business service teams. **Accessibility** If you require any accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact us and we will work with you to ensure the best possible candidate experience. We value a diverse and inclusive workforce and are an equal opportunities employer.

title: QC BioAnalytical Sciences
description: **QC BioAnalytical Sciences** This job specification outlines the general responsibilities associated with the role of QC BioAnalytical Sciences \- Senior Associate on the Client Manufacturing site. This role will be responsible for leading, coordinating, facilitating, and undertaking activities pertaining to lab operations and compliance, with minimal supervision, acting as a role model for other lab colleagues. The role undertakes implementing continuous improvement projects and supporting a lean culture and Right First Time (RFT). The role also represents the QC unit by liaising with internal and external customers. Additionally, the role may involve carrying out additional work functions that are not described in this specification but are associated with their role. **Key Responsibilities:** * Experience in a wide variety of analytical techniques including but not limited to cell based\- Bioassays, Immunoassays, Cell Culture (use of cells to execute bioassays) and Aseptic Technique. Experience with liquid\-handling robots is also preferable. * Plan and perform multiple, complex routine/ non\-routine methods and procedures and a large variety of assays. * Participate in the peer review of analytical data. * Responsible for providing technical guidance and applying expertise and critical thinking to help to resolve technical issues. * Lead training of staff on technical aspects of job as required. * Develop, revise and implement procedures that comply with appropriate regulatory requirements. * Qualification of analytical equipment and related testing functions. * Participate in Analytical Method Transfers * Compliance with Standard Operating Procedures and Registered specifications. * Ensure the laboratory is operated in a safe manner * Maintain quality systems within the QC laboratories to ensure ongoing compliance to cGLP and cGMP. * Ensure timely completion of Laboratory Investigation Reports and deviations through the Non\-Conformance procedures. * Participate in the generation and update of SOP's, trend data, investigations, non\-conformances, validation protocols, reports, method validation/verifications and equipment qualifications. * Participate in regulatory agency inspections as required. * Plan and implement procedures and systems to maximise operating efficiency. * Manage and contribute to the achievements of department productivity and quality goals. Requirements * Bachelors degree in a science discipline * Microbiology degree acceptable, only if clear and direct experience with bioassays/immunoassays is present * Strong experience in Cell based potency bioassays min experience, Cell culture, GMP/GDP Elisa ( minimum 6 months, ideally over 1 year) * Hands\-on cell culture skills * Direct experience with immunoassay techniques, especially ELISA * Familiarity with GMP/GDP lab environments * Prior involvement in bioassays/immunoassays, not entry\-level (someone who can hit the ground running with minimal ramp\-up) * CVs should evidence routine lab testing, method execution, data analysis for the above * Experience in additional project\-based lab responsibilities (e.g., Lean/CI, error management, SOPs, method transfers) * Comfort with digital lab systems (e.g., Lens Smart Lab) * Experience with multi\-functional teams in a large QC setting

title: QC Microbiology Analyst
description: I am recruiting for a QC Microbiology Analyst to join a leading biopharmaceutical company located in North Dublin. The analyst will be responsible for supporting Drug Substance and Drug Product site activities related to Bioburden and Endotoxin in\-process testing and sample processing. **Key Duties and Responsibilities** * Carrying out In\-Process testing of critical lot related samples to support Manufacturing activities. * Performing Bioburden testing on Utility samples, Drug Substance and Raw Materials within required hold times. * Performing Bacterial Endotoxin Testing on Utility samples, Drug Substance and Drug Product within required hold times * Performing Growth Promotion testing of media lots entering the laboratory. * Carrying out identifications of Microorganisms isolated post testing activities. * Carrying out the Minute Mouse Virus by PCR for in\-process samples. * Performing compendial verification, method transfer/validation, and routine testing of in\-process, drug substance and drug product samples in accordance with applicable procedures and cGMP requirements. * Completing all laboratory documentation in a timely and accurate manner. * Compliance with Standard Operating Procedures and Registered Specifications. * Assisting in authoring and reviewing documentation. * Providing support to other departments to ensure qualification and production schedules are adhered to. * Reviewing paperwork, and reconciliation of analysis performed in the laboratory. * Ensuring that any alert/action limit excursions are investigated according to site procedures. * Adherence to and improvement upon lean lab and 6S practices. * Ensuring a high standard of housekeeping and safety is maintained in the laboratory. * Performing any other activities as indicated by the Microbiology Manager. **Qualifications and Experience** * The ideal candidate should hold a minimum of a Level 8 degree in Microbiology or related discipline. * At least 1 years’ experience in a pharmaceutical / healthcare laboratory or related technical function. * A strong background in Microbiology and aseptic technique is desirable but not essential. * Sterility experience is desirable but not essential. * Experience in Bioburden and Endotoxin testing is desirable but not essential. * Experience using MODA, CIMS, LIMS, LES and Veeva Vault is desirable but not essential. * The successful candidate must demonstrate excellent written and verbal communication skills. * The ability to work in a team based collaborative environment is essential.

title: Quality Assurance Specialist
description: As a Quality Assurance professional at Manley, you will play a crucial role in ensuring quality excellence across various live projects. In this position, you will manage the quality interests of the company, ensuring that all projects are executed to the highest standards. Your work will primarily take place at our head office, with regular visits to project sites for meetings and assessments. standards * Able to review MARs and ensure compliance with specifications and technical submittals and method statements * Experienced in developing registers, documentation and reviewing information. title: Quantity Surveyor

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Cork or Dublin.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 80 years, guiding how we shape a better world. **Driving Commercial Excellence** Our Procurement and Cost Management team is redefining how value is delivered across infrastructure and building projects. By combining deep technical expertise with a collaborative mindset, we’re helping clients make smarter investment decisions and embed sustainability, innovation, and behavioral best practice into every stage of the project lifecycle. **The Opportunity** Join Arup’s growing Project \& Commercial Management (PCM) team in Cork, where you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping the future of cost and commercial advisory across Ireland. As a Quantity Surveyor, you’ll help lead a dynamic, multidisciplinary team focused on delivering high\-impact infrastructure and building projects that prioritise sustainability, value, and innovation. This is a chance to influence how we deliver commercial excellence, from mentoring junior colleagues and refining procurement strategies, to driving whole\-life value and embedding behavioural best practice into day\-to\-day operations. You’ll be part of a team that’s not only expanding in size, but also in ambition with opportunities to contribute to strategic growth, thought leadership, and the evolution of Arup’s commercial capability. **As a Quantity Surveyor At Arup, You Will** * Support the preparation of cost estimates, cost plans, and tender documentation across infrastructure and building projects to help inform project budgets and procurement decisions. * Assist with procurement and contract administration activities, including preparing tender documents, reviewing contractor submissions, measuring works, and supporting valuations. * Contribute to cost control and commercial reporting by helping prepare cost updates, forecasts, and financial summaries that support effective project management. * Work closely with multidisciplinary teams, attending meetings, site visits, and workshops to build understanding of project delivery, stakeholder needs, and commercial priorities at different project stages. * Develop practical experience in construction contracts (e.g., NEC, Public Works Contracts, FIDIC) while supporting senior colleagues in delivering high\-quality commercial outputs and maintaining accurate project records. **Does this role inspire you?** **We’re Looking For Someone Who** * Has relevant qualifications in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline, with early career experience welcomed. * Has a basic understanding of cost management principles, procurement processes, and contract administration. * Is keen to develop technical and commercial skills through hands\-on project experience. * Demonstrates strong numerical, analytical, and organisational skills, with attention to detail. * Has good communication skills and the ability to work effectively within a team environment. * Has familiarity with MS Office applications and an interest in developing skills in estimating tools (e.g. CostX, CostOS) **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Well\-being and Culture: at Arup, your well\-being matters. We foster a culture of care, inclusion, and collaboration, supported by global initiatives and local programmes that promote mental health, social connection, and a sense of belonging. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please contact recruitment.europe@arup.com . We're here to support you. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?** https://www.arup.com/careers/

title: Real Estate Solicitor | Permanent | Dublin
description: After a sustained period of significant growth, the real estate team at Philip Lee LLP is looking to hire a 1\-3PQE solicitor. **The Firm** Philip Lee LLP is one of Ireland’s leading commercial law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London and New York. The firm has built a team of top tier lawyers who can advise on every aspect of business, both in Ireland and internationally. The firm acts for companies across all industry sectors and the lawyers at the firm are recognised for their ability to work through complex challenges and provide innovative solutions. Philip Lee LLP is the sole Irish member of Multilaw, a consortium network of over 10,000 lawyers in 100 countries worldwide. **The Role** **Key Responsibilities:** * Assisting with advising property teams on the acquisition and funding of land for major residential and commercial developments. * Assisting with advising developers on the legal delivery of turnkey and forward\-funded developments. * Assisting with advising developers and landowners in judicial review proceedings challenging planning decisions. * Assisting with advising on challenges to the inclusion of lands on Residential Zoned Land Tax (RZLT) maps. * Assisting with the structuring of multi\-unit residential and mixed\-use developments, including management company and service charge regimes. * Assisting with complex High Court and Commercial Court litigation arising from development, land use, and property disputes. * Assisting with the negotiation of complex settlements in multi\-party property and planning disputes to achieve commercially workable outcomes. * Assisting with advising borrowers and developers on restructuring, forbearance, and distressed property scenarios. **About You** The successful candidate will: * be a 1\-3 PQE solicitor with experience in real estate; * be naturally collaborative and understand the importance of being part of a team; * prioritise attention to detail; * display commercial awareness and understand what is important to clients; and * have strong communication skills. **Why Apply?** This is an excellent opportunity to join a growing real estate practice. The firm offers a collaborative and inclusive culture with a genuine focus on professional development and progression. **Benefits** At Philip Lee LLP we are committed to maintaining an inclusive, supportive environment in which all of our people feel valued and can enjoy doing what they love, working together to provide a first\-class service to our clients. Specific initiatives to support our employees include but are not limited to: * associate and partner development programmes; * the opportunity to join various firm committees, including our social, wellness, green, pro bono and diversity committees; * subsidised yoga and fitness classes; and * numerous social events throughout the year. Please submit your CV in confidence to hoshaughnessy@philiplee.ie.

title: Receptionist
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role** We are currently hiring a full\-time Receptionist to play a key role in supporting our operations in our office in Dublin 15\. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * First point of contact for all visitors. * Answering incoming calls in a friendly, professional, and knowledgeable manner, taking message, or dealing with simple enquiries. * Providing administrative and clerical tasks such as scanning or printing, etc. * Maintaining Company filing systems, ensuring all documentation is filed in a timely manner. * Arranging meetings, appointments. * Adhering to GDPR confidentiality. * Providing exceptional administrative and organisational support to the management team. * Assisting with other duties as delegated by the Management Team. **The Candidate:** **Required Experience and Competencies:** * Some experience working in administration, front house or reception role * Excellent communication skills. * Motivation to achieve positive results and meet deadlines. * Excellent telephone manner. * Strong interpersonal and organizational skills. * Strong attention to details. * Ability to multi\-task and act in a confidential manner is a must. * Excellent IT and computer skills \- In particular, experience using excel, word and email. * A valid full driver's license and own car to perform an ad hoc duties in the local territory. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Refrigeration Technician / HVAC Technician
description: 🔧 **Refrigeration / HVAC Technician – Dublin** I’m currently working with a leading engineering services provider looking to bring on a **Refrigeration / HVAC Technician** to join a site\-based team in Dublin. This is a great opportunity to work within a structured environment supporting the **maintenance, fault\-finding, and operation of critical HVAC and refrigeration systems** . 🛠️ **Key Responsibilities** * Carry out planned and reactive maintenance across HVAC and refrigeration systems * Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical, and control\-related faults * Ensure systems are operating efficiently and in line with safety standards * Support the overall reliability and performance of site equipment * Complete relevant documentation and work within site procedures ✅ **Requirements** * Minimum 3\+ years’ hands‑on experience working in an industrial or critical environment (e.g. pharmaceutical, manufacturing, data centre, utilities, or similar regulated sites) * Proven experience in refrigeration and HVAC service, maintenance, or commissioning * Strong fault‑finding and troubleshooting capability * F‑Gas certification * Experience working on fixed plant systems such as chillers, cold rooms, pack systems, AHUs, or similar * Ability to work independently within a site‑based role 🎯 **What’s on offer** * 💰 Competitive salary package * 🏥 Full VHI healthcare cover * 📈 Pension contribution * 📚 Ongoing training and development * 🔒 Long\-term, stable opportunity 🕐 Roles are available across both **shift\-based schedules** and **Monday–Friday day roles** , depending on your preference. ***If this sounds like something you’d be open to, feel free to reach out or send me your CV — happy to run through it with you 👍***

title: Registered Nurse
description: **Are you passionate about dementia care?** We are seeking to appoint Staff Nurses **The successful candidates should be flexible and committed and fulfil the following criteria:** * Current registration on the General / Psychiatric division of the register maintained by the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland (NMBI) * Have **three years of experience** in a nursing role **in Irelands Healthcare System** * Current experience in the care of the elderly and people living with dementia * Comprehensive understanding of relevant clinical standards * Demonstrate excellent communication (both verbal and written), organisational skills, flexibility and commitment * A post\-graduate diploma in gerontology or dementia is desirable. **Please note we will not consider, and cannot respond to, your application unless you meet ALL the criteria outlined above.** **Responsibilities:** The staff nurse will Practice Nursing according to the: * Professional Code of Practice as laid down by the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland (NMBI) * Nurses and Midwives Act 2011 (NMBI) * Professional Clinical Guidelines * Health Act 2007 (Regulations 2013\) \& National Quality Standards for Residential Care for Older People in Ireland 2016\. * St. John of God Order Policies, Saint Josephs Shankill Policies and Procedures and best evidenced practice. * Current legislation The staff nurse will: * Be competent to deal with any allegation, suspected or confirmed abuse of any resident in accordance with the Safeguarding Vulnerable Adults Policy * Follow appropriate lines of authority within the Nurse Management structure. * Participate as an inter\-disciplinary team member in all aspects of residents care. * Demonstrate a high degree of motivation and assume responsibility for their own professional development and contribute to the development of others * Promote good interpersonal relationships with residents, their named representative, their family/social network supports and the interdisciplinary care team in accordance with ethical standards. * Demonstrate flexibility by assisting in other areas of the centre as required e.g. Day Care Unit * Participate in regular performance review with their line manager, identifying key performance objectives and appropriate plans/measures to achieve areas for improvement and appropriate plans/measures to achieve them in a supportive environment. * Respect and maintain the privacy, dignity and confidentiality of the residents and their relatives, in relation to all the centres activities. * Professional Expectations: The nurse must comply with the Code of Professional Conduct as laid down by the NMBI. The nursing profession demands a high standard of professional behaviour from its members and each registered nurse is accountable for his or her practice. The nurse must work within his/her scope of practice and must take measures to develop and maintain the competence necessary for professional practice. The nurse must be aware of ethical policies and procedures which pertain to his/her area of practice. Residents confidentiality must be maintained at all times. **Clinical Responsibilities** * Assess, plan, implement and evaluate individualised residents care within the agreed nursing model. * Collaborate with the interdisciplinary team in the co\-ordination and delivery of residents care. * Be an advocate for the individual residents and for his/her family/named representative. * Ensure that care is carried out in an empathetic and ethical manner and that the dignity of the resident is given proper consideration. * Collaborate with the resident, their family/named representative, and the inter\-disciplinary team to facilitate appropriate continuity of care. * Maintain appropriate and accurate written records and reports regarding residents care in accordance with local guidelines and within current legislative requirements. * Ensure that current legal requirements for the safe administration and storage of medicines are adhered to in accordance with best practice. * Participate in innovation and change in the approach to residents care delivery, and contribute to service planning process, based on best practice and under the direction of nursing management, particularly in relation to new research findings and advances in treatment. * Have a sound knowledge of clinical standards and participate in residents care audits. * Participate in the setting of professional standards. Understand and co\-operate with the need to measure and audit the effectiveness of care given in order to provide quality care. * Participate in the setting of professional standards. Understand and co\-operate with the need to measure and audit the effectiveness of care given. * Participate in research programmes and understand the need for evidence\-based practice. * Participate in clinical audit and review. **All applications for Saint Josephs Centre, Shankill are submitted through our official Rezoomo hiring page. We do not request payment for screening, medical checks or vetting at any stage of recruitment. We are not recruiting internationally at this time.**
